subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following groups is paid first when a firm liquidates?
explanation: When a firm liquidates, the debenture holders are paid first. Debenture holders are creditors of the company and have a higher claim on the assets and earnings than the shareholders. This is because debentures are a type of debt instrument, unlike shares, which represent ownership in the company. Therefore, in the event of liquidation, the company has a legal obligation to pay its debts before it pays out any ownership interests.

subject: Commerce
q:
MBV Enterprises
Balance sheet as at 31st December, 1992
Liabilities # Assets # #
Capital Fixed Assets
Balance as at 1 1992 60000 fittings 9200
Net profit 5250 vehicles 7500 16700
65250
Less Drawings 6600
58650
Current Assets
Stock in hand 8800
Debtors 15500
Current Liabilities Cash at bank 21000
Creditors 9730 Cash in hand 6380 51680
68380 68380


What is the circulating capital of the sole proprietor?
explanation: The circulating capital of a sole proprietor is also known as working capital or current capital. It is calculated as the difference between current assets and current liabilities. In this case, the current assets are Stock in hand (8800), Debtors (15500), Cash at bank (21000), and Cash in hand (6380), which sum up to 51680. The current liabilities are Creditors (9730). Therefore, the circulating capital is 51680 - 9730 = 41950. However, the question asks for the total current assets, not the difference between current assets and current liabilities. Therefore, the correct answer is 51680.

subject: Commerce
q: A company a total revenue of #108million with a total cost of #91 million in 1991. if it paid 45% tax on its gross profit for that year, determine its net profit
explanation:
Gross profit = total revenue - total cost
= 108m - 91m
= #17m

Tax = 45% x gross profit
= 45% x 17
= #7.65

Net profit = #17m - #7.65
= #9.35m

subject: Commerce

explanation: A sole enterprise, also known as a sole proprietorship, is a type of business that is owned and run by one individual. This type of business structure may flourish best in a retailing business where the scale of operations is small to medium and the owner can manage all aspects of the business. Mining, oil exploration, and car assembly businesses typically require large capital investments and complex operations that may be beyond the capacity of a sole proprietorship.

explanation: Vending machine is a retail outlet that automatically dispenses are pre-packed items such as drinks stored in it whenever the appropriate value coin is inserted. In other words, vending machine involves the sale of goods to ultimate customers through coin-operated machines.

subject: Commerce
q: The difference between the total payments for imports and the receipts from exports within a given period is refered to as
explanation: The difference between the total payments for imports and the receipts from exports within a given period is referred to as the 'balance of trade'. This term refers to the difference in value over a period of time of a country's imports and exports of merchandise. A positive balance of trade is known as a trade surplus and consists of exporting more than your imports, a negative balance of trade is known as a trade deficit or, informally, a trade gap.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : BALANCE OF PAYMENT

explanation: A proforma invoice is a preliminary invoice that is sent to a buyer before a sale is confirmed. After the buyer receives and review the proforma invoice, the seller proceeds to send a sales invoice and starts working on their part of the deal.

all ships must be insured at every journey
all ships are insured separately from the crew

explanation: The statement 'all ships normally arrive at their destination with goods' is true about sea transport. Sea transport is a method of conveyance of goods via waterways. Freight ships or cargo ships transport goods from one place to another. The other options are not necessarily true for all ships. Not all ships have specific routes to ply, not all ships must be insured at every journey, and not all ships are insured separately from the crew.

q: A sole proprietor insured his goods worth #200,000 for half the value. There was a fire incident in which goods valued #80000 were destroyed. The value of compensation expected from the insurer is
explanation: This is a case of under-insurance in insurance terms. When the insured value is less than the actual value, the insurer will only pay compensation in proportion to the insured value. In this case, the goods were insured for half their value, so the insurer will only pay half the value of the destroyed goods. Therefore, the compensation expected from the insurer is #40,000.

explanation: The business in the stock exchange is characterized essentially by speculations. explanation: In an agency by agreement, consent must be present. This means that both parties, the agent and the principal, agree to the terms of the agency relationship. Authority is given to the agent by the principal as part of the agency agreement, but it is not a requirement for the agreement to exist.

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following maxims negate the principle of collective bargaining?
options: individual employees have weak bargaining base
individual employees attain strength when they come together in union
only members of the union can benefit from agreements negotiated by the union
collective bargaining covers the vast majority of employees, whether union members or not
explanation: The maxim 'individual employees have weak bargaining base' negates the principle of collective bargaining. Collective bargaining is a process of negotiation between employers and a group of employees aimed at agreements to regulate working salaries, working conditions, benefits, and other aspects of workers' compensation and rights. The employees rely on a trade union representative to bargain for them and are bound by the agreements. This principle is based on the idea that a group has more negotiating power than an individual. Therefore, the idea that individual employees have a weak bargaining base is contrary to the principle of collective bargaining.

q:
Use the conversion below to answer question below

MUSA: Taiwo why are you in business?

TAIWO: To optimized profit, provide quality product and discharge my social responsibilities. What of you, Okon and Kalu

OKON: I am in business to cater for the need of the consumers at all costs.

KALU: I am in business to make profit, provide quality product at reasonable prices and establish a good reputation for myself.

The business that may end up not discharging the social responsibility is
explanation: From the conversation, Okon is the only one who did not mention discharging social responsibilities as part of his business objectives. He only mentioned catering for the needs of consumers at all costs. Social responsibility in business refers to the obligation of a business to act in ways that benefit society. It includes activities like philanthropy, volunteering, and ethical labour practices.

the consumers have no medium for expressing their feelings
explanation: The activities of consumers association in Nigeria have not been effective because there is poor enlightenment about consumer rights. Consumer rights are the rights given to a "consumer" to protect him/her from being cheated by salesman/manufacturer. Consumer protection laws are designed to ensure fair trade competition and the free flow of truthful information in the marketplace. However, if consumers are not aware of these rights, they cannot effectively exercise them or seek redress when these rights are violated.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: GOVERNMENT REGULATION : CONSUMER PROTECTION

subject: Commerce
q: The least of the advantages of indigenization is that
options: the standard of living of the indigenes is improved
the economy is controlled by the nationals
explanation: Indigenization, which typically involves transferring ownership and control of businesses or industries from foreign ownership to domestic ownership, can have several advantages, but avoiding foreigners' domination is not a primary goal or advantage of this policy.

topics.title: BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT AND SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY : FACTORS

subject: Commerce
q: The main objective of a trade association can best be described as promoting and protecting
explanation: As per Sydney and Beatrice Webb's "a trade union is a continuous association of wage earners for the purpose of maintaining and improving the conditions of their working lives". The primary objective of trade unions is to promote and protect the interests of its members.

topics.title: TRADE ASSOCIATION : MEANING & FUNCTIONS

subject: Commerce
q: The management function of evaluating whether objectives are being achieved is
explanation: Control is a fundamental managerial function or process which measures current performance and guides it towards some predetermined objectives. The essence of control lies in checking progress against plans, setting up individual and organizational performance standards, and seeing that they are achieved as per the plan.

topics.title: ELEMENTS OF BUSINESS MANAGEMENT : PRINCIPLE

subject: Commerce
q: In a business organization , the annual operation plan or budget is a form of
explanation: An annual operating plan is a written report that describes the finances, budgets, key performance indicators, and other resources required to meet short-term business goals. It is used as the foundation to carry out daily business activities, pinpointing the projects and objectives required to meet key targets.

topics.title: ELEMENTS OF BUSINESS MANAGEMENT : BUSINESS RESOURCES

subject: Commerce
q: A key advantage of delegation is that
options: the manager has less work to do
the manager spends less time on planning
explanation: Delegation is a means of co­ordination between superiors and subordinates. It brings close each other and develops cordial relation among them. It helps in the establishment of industrial peace and harmony.

topics.title: ELEMENTS OF BUSINESS MANAGEMENT : PRINCIPLE

subject: Commerce
q: Social responsibility of business requires enterprises to
options: hold annual social parties for the public
draw their workers only from the immediate environment
address themselves to issues relating to the society
set up social welfare centers in their areas of operation
explanation: The obligation of any business to protect and serve public interest is known as social responsibility of business. Social responsibility is an ethical or theory that an entity, be it an organization or individual, has an obligation to act to benefit society at large.

topics.title: BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT AND SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: The existing system of governance and the administrative sub-divisions of a country which affect the operations of business in the country is described as
explanation: The political environment is one of the external environmental factors which affect the business. It can impact positively or negatively depending on the situation, business, and many other factors.

topics.title: BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT AND SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY : FACTORS

subject: Commerce
q: Sudden technology changes can have the effect of making
options: the motivational factors in the company difficult to monitor
explanation: Technological advances that improve production efficiency will shift a supply curve to the right. The cost of production goes down, and consumers will demand more of the product at lower prices. Computers, televisions and photographic equipment are good examples of the effects of technology on a supply curve. Changes in technology can affect the demand for different products or the demand for related products. It can increase the market for a product by increasing the demand for a new product and making an older product obsolete.

topics.title: BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT AND SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY : FACTORS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following contributed least to the evolution of commercial activities in Nigeria?
explanation: The development of trader's unions contributed least to the evolution of commercial activities in Nigeria. The development of banks, transportation, and currencies had a more direct and significant impact on commercial activities. Banks provide the necessary financial services for commerce, transportation facilitates the movement of goods and services, and currencies enable transactions. Trader's unions, while important, do not have as direct or broad an impact on commercial activities as the other options.

topics.title: INTRODUCTION TO COMMERCE : MEANING & SCOPE

subject: Commerce
q: One of the factors which critically determines the choice of occupation is
explanation:
Interest refers to a person's enthusiasm or inclination towards a particular field or activity. It plays a crucial role in guiding individuals towards occupations that they find personally fulfilling and enjoyable.

Interest often serves as the primary motivation for pursuing a specific career path. It reflects one's passion and willingness to invest time and effort into developing the necessary skills and expertise for a particular occupation.

topics.title: OCCUPATION : MEANING & IMPORTANCE

subject: Commerce
q: Extractive industries may also be denominated as
explanation: The extractive industries are those engage in obtaining raw materials and natural resources from the soil or sea. It is also referred to as the primary stage of production e.g farming, fishing, mining etc.

topics.title: OCCUPATION : TYPES OF OCCUPATION

subject: Commerce
q: The primary objective of a business organization is to
options: Cater for the needs of its owners
Assist the government to serve the citizens
Improve the standard of living of its proprietors
explanation: The primary objective of any business is to earn a profit. Profit is necessary for growing and expanding business activities.

topics.title: ELEMENTS OF BUSINESS MANAGEMENT : BUSINESS RESOURCES

subject: Commerce
q: Service which are of absolute monopoly can can best be provided by
explanation: Services that are of absolute monopoly are best provided by public enterprises. An absolute monopoly is a market structure where a single entity is the only provider of a particular good or service. Public enterprises are government-owned entities that are often established to provide essential public services, including those that are natural monopolies. A natural monopoly occurs when a single firm can serve the entire market demand at a lower cost than any combination of two or more firms. This is often the case with utilities like water, electricity, and public transportation.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PUBLIC CORPORATION : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: One advantage the co-operative society has over the company is that the
options: The latter pays tax while the former does not
Former attract more capitals than the later
Members of the former is usually more than the latter
Former, unlike the latter, has limited liabilities
explanation: The primary objective of a cooperative society is not to make profit but to attain the common goal of its members. One of the advantage is that Cooperative societies are exempted from paying taxes in most of the states, since their primary interest is not necessarily profit-making.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PARTNERSHIP : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the above information below to answer question below
Mr obi is a manager of obi Agricultural Venture. He makes all the decisions and takes all profits from the business. He had agreed with Alhaji Musa. It can be deduce from the above that obi Agricultural Venture is (a farmer) to pool their resources together and expand their business.They invited Mr Abayomi (a cotton grower) to join them. Mr Abayami agreed on the condition that his liability in the new firm will be limited to his investment in it. They thus registered Obi, Musa and Abayaomi's Agricultural ventures.

It can be deduced from the above that obi agricultural venture is ?
explanation: The business is a sole proprietorship since Mr Obi is the owner of the business is the business is not converted to any other form of business.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PARTNERSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the above information below to answer question below
Mr obi is a manager of obi Agricultural Venture. He makes all the decisions and takes all profits from the business. He had agreed with Alhaji Musa. It can be deduce from the above that obi Agricultural Venture is (a farmer) to pool their resources together and expand their business.They invited Mr Abayomi (a cotton grower) to join them. Mr Abayami agreed on the condition that his liability in the new firm will be limited to his investment in it. They thus registered Obi, Musa and Abayaomi's Agricultural ventures.
which of the statements below is true of Mr. Abayomi?
options: he can assign his shares to another without consent of other partners
He cannot take part in the management of the business
His actions as a limited partner can bind the firm
explanation: According to the information given, Mr. Abayomi agreed to join the venture on the condition that his liability in the new firm will be limited to his investment in it. This is characteristic of a limited partner in a limited partnership. A limited partner is an investor who does not have management control and cannot be held liable for more than their investment in the partnership. Therefore, the correct answer is 'He cannot take part in the management of the business'.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PARTNERSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: A source of capital to private limited companies is shares issued to
explanation: A private company is normally restricted to issuing shares to its members, to staff and their families and to debenture holders. However, by private arrangement, the company may issue shares to anyone it chooses. Shares in a private limited company may only be sold or transferred with the permission of the directors.

topics.title: FINANCING BUSINESS : TYPES OF CAPITAL

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is an example of a conglomerate?
explanation: Conglomerate is the combination of two or more business entities engaged in either entirely different or similar businesses that fall under one corporate group, usually involving a parent company and many subsidiaries.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: MERGER : MEANING AND TYPES OF MERGER

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following statement is true of preferred stockholders?
options: They have no voting rights at annual general meetings
They first claim company asset before creditors
The have first claim to company asset after all debts have been settled
They share dividends equally with common shareholders
explanation: Preferred stockholders generally do not have voting rights, as common stockholders do, but they have a greater claim to the company's assets.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES : FEATURES AND IMPORTANCE

subject: Commerce

explanation: Pre-emptive right give a shareholder the opportunity to buy additional shares in any future issue of a company's common stock before the shares are made available to the general public.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES : FEATURES AND IMPORTANCE

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the information below to answer this questions below

SONU ENTERPRISES
Balance Sheet as at 31/3/98
# #
Capital 6000.00 Plant/Machinery 2300.00
Net profit 2000.00 Motor van 2000.00
drawings(137.50) Stock 1250.00
Creditors 1000.00 debtors 480.00
Accruals 187.50 Bank 3000.00
Cash 20.00


What is the current ratio?
explanation:
Current ratio = Current asset/ Current liabilities

Current assets = stock + debtors + bank + cash
= 1250 + 480 + 3000 + 20
= 4750

Current liabilities = creditors + Accruals
= 1000 + 187.50
= 1187.5

Current ratio = 4750/1187.5

= 4/1 = 4: 1

topics.title: FINANCING BUSINESS : TURNOVER

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the information below to answer this questions below

SONU ENTERPRISES
Balance Sheet as at 31/3/98
# #
Capital 6000.00 Plant/Machinery 2300.00
Net profit 2000.00 Motor van 2000.00
drawings(137.50) Stock 1250.00
Creditors 1000.00 debtors 480.00
Accruals 187.50 Bank 3000.00
Cash 20.00

What is the acid ratio?
explanation:
= Current assets - Stock/ Current liabilities

= 4750 - 1250/ 1187.5

= 3500/1187.5

= 2.95 : 1

topics.title: FINANCING BUSINESS : TURNOVER

explanation: Liquid capital is any capital that can be easily transform to a desired form e.g cash, bank, debtors etc.

topics.title: FINANCING BUSINESS : TYPES OF CAPITAL

subject: Commerce
q: If a firm's turnover is N15,000 and the cost of goods sold is N 10,000 What is the percentage of gross profit on sales?
explanation:
Gross profit = Turnover/ sales - Cost of goods sold
= 15,000 - 10,000
= #5,000

Gross profit on sales = Gross profit / Sales x 100

= 5000/15000 x 100

= 33.3%

explanation: Vending machine is a machine from which products are sold. The customer puts money in a slot, selects a product from the menu and the product is dispensed from the machine.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : PUBLIC RELATION & CUSTOMER SERVICE

q: What document is required when a country imposes ad valorem import duties on goods?
explanation: The main purpose of a consular invoice is to provide a complete and detailed description of the goods to the foreign customs authority, so as to ensure that the correct import duty is levied on the goods

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : DOCMENT USED IN INTERNATION TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Entre port trade is said to have taken place when goods are?
options: Held at the port payment of custom duty
Sold in the ship under special regulation
explanation: Entre port refers to a port, or warehouse where goods are imported to be stored or traded for re-export.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : FOREIGN TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: When a price is quoted at F.O.B it means that the price?
options: excludes loading charges incurred in bringing the goods to port of destination
exclude all necessary shipping charges payable for insurance
includes all charges payable on shipping a consignment to the port destination
includes charges payable for loading goods onto the ship
explanation: F.O.B (Free On Board) is a term in international commercial law specifying at what point respective obligations, costs, and risk involved in the delivery of goods shift from the seller to the buyer. When a price is quoted at FOB, it means that the price includes charges payable for loading goods onto the ship. It does not exclude loading charges incurred in bringing the goods to port of destination, exclude all necessary shipping charges payable for insurance, or include all charges payable on shipping a consignment to the port destination.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : DOCMENT USED IN INTERNATION TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Emeka ordered goods for his retail shop. The manufacturer sells the goods at retail price of #500 but would give it to Emeka at #400. What kind of discount did he get?
explanation: A trade discount is a reduction in price given by a manufacturer or wholesaler to a retailer. In this case, Emeka is a retailer who is given a reduction in price by the manufacturer. Therefore, the kind of discount he got is a trade discount.

topics.title: PURCHASES AND SALES OF GOODS : TERMS OF TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Nigerian Postal Services (NIPOST) facilitates commerce in all the following ways except through
explanation: NIPOST helps to make the remittance of trade values in terms of money order, postal order stamps, and financial services more convenient. Nigeria's postal service (NIPOST) is one of the nation's core industry that needs to be urgently upgraded to be more productive, efficient and customer responsive.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: COMMUNICATION : ADVANTAGES & DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: The document with which a manufacturing enterprises releases stock of raw materials to the factory for product is called?
explanation: A materials release note is a document used by manufacturing enterprises to release stock of raw materials from the warehouse or strong area to the factory for production. It serves as an authorization for the movement of materials and typically includes details such as the type of materials, quantity released, date, and any relevant instructions or references. This document helps ensure proper inventory management and control within the manufacturing process.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: WAREHOUSING: : TYPES & IMPORTANCE

subject: Commerce
q: The two main categories under which marine losses fall into are?
options: Voyage policy loss and time policy loss
explanation: Marine losses can be categorised into total loss ( which is subdivided into: actual and constructive loss) and partial loss ( which is divided into: general average loss and particular average loss).

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : PRINCIPLES

subject: Commerce
q: Insurance companies operate on the principal of indemnity. This means that an insured person or firm collector
options: Double the value of the losses suffered
The total sum of the premium paid prior to the loss
Damage claims equal to the loss suffered.
explanation: Indemnity is the compensation given to the insured by the insurer in the event of his suffering a loss. Under this principle, the insured will be given compensation for loss suffered and he will be restored to his former position before the loss ocurred.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : PRINCIPLES

subject: Commerce
q: The most important difference between community banks and peoples' bank is that the?
options: Latter is owned solely by government while the former are not
Former cater for ruler dwellers while the latter cater for both rural and urban dwellers
Former are service oriented while the latter are profit oriented
Latter advances loans to the public while former advance loans to the rural dwellers only.
explanation: The main difference between community banks and people's banks is in their ownership structure. Community banks are typically owned by the community members and operate for the benefit of the community. On the other hand, people's banks are government-owned institutions. Therefore, the latter is owned solely by the government while the former are not.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : TYPES OF BANK

subject: Commerce
q: The assets, liabilities and general management of a distressed bank may be taken over by?
explanation: Nigeria Banks Deposit Insurance Corporation is an independent agency of the Federal Government of Nigeria. The purpose of the deposit insurance system is to protect depositors and guarantee the settlement of insured funds when a deposit-taking financial institution can no longer repay their deposits, thereby helping to maintain financial system stability.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES & CHALLEGES

subject: Commerce
q: The type of securities that the stock exchange deals with are
options: Warrants, currency notes, money orders and postal orders
Cheques, bank drafts , warrants and promissory notes
Bonds ivory, treasure bills, debentures and warrants
explanation: The stock exchange primarily deals with securities such as stocks, shares, bonds and warrants. Stocks and shares represent ownership in a company, bonds are debt securities issued by companies or governments to raise capital, and warrants are derivatives that give the holder the right to buy the underlying security at a specific price, on or before a certain date.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE : TYPES OF SECURITIES

subject: Commerce
q:
i. Companies must be registered as private limited liability companies.
ii. Companies must not have less than 100 shareholders
iii. No shareholder may have more than 75% of issued shared capital.

Which of the above are requirements for admission into the second-tier securities market?
explanation:
The requirement for admission are:
- the company must be incorporated as a public limited liability company under the Companies Act 1968
- the shareholders must not less than 100
- the company must sign a general undertaking with the stock exchange market
- the company must make available, at least 10% of its equity share to the investing public
- the financial statements of the company for three years must be submitted to the stock exchange
- any investor or shareholder will not be allowed to either directly or indirectly acquire more than 75% of the issued capital.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE : SECOND- TIER SECURITIES MARKET

subject: Commerce
q: An underlined principle in the marketing of goods is that firms should
explanation: Every business needs a marketing strategy. It introduces the company's goods and services to potential customers and informs existing ones of additional opportunities to buy.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : THE MARKETING CONCEPT

subject: Commerce
q: Product X was introduced into the market at N4.00 with its cost of product at N3.90 while competing products are at N4.50. What pricing policy are producers of X adopting?
explanation: Market penetration is defined as a measurement, is the assessment of how much a product is being sold relative to the total estimated market for that product, expressed as a percentage. It involves reducing the price in the market in order to gain instant dominance in the market.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

subject: Commerce

explanation: Personal selling involves the direct personal contact of sellers with the potential buyers with view to making sales. It can also be referred to as the face-to-face meeting of the salesmen with the potential buyers in their houses, offices, or market place e.g door-to-door selling.

explanation: Public relations is the image building done by an organisation to give the public favourable impression about its aims and policies.

explanation: An agent must not make secret profit and he if does, the principal must be aware. However, the principal can pay the agent the commission or other agreed remuneration, indemnify the agent for any expenses of losses incurred by the agents, provide all necessary facilities to the agent for proper execution of the contract.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : ELEMENT OF A VALID CONTRACT

subject: Commerce
q: Sabo and Gambo entered into a contract for the sale of two baskets of tomatoes belonging to Gambo. Before the date the date of delivery and without any default by Gambo, the tomatoes were stolen. This is an example of termination of contract by
explanation: A contract can be discharged when the subject matter or basis of the contract has been destroyed or frustrated by an act of God e.g accident,flood, war etc.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : MEANING AND TYPES OF CONTRACT

Is young in the eye of the law
May not be able to write the contract
explanation: Minor is an infant who is under 18 years of age. A contract entered with an infant or minor is not valid in the court of law.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : ELEMENT OF A VALID CONTRACT

subject: Commerce
q: A person who is cheated in business transaction can seek legal remedy
options: Even if the agreement is verbal and the amount involved is large
Only if the business is a sole proprietorship whose owner is known
Regardless of the form of business organization
Only if the transaction is documented in a contract
explanation: A person who is cheated in a business transaction can seek legal remedy regardless of the form of business organization. This is because the law protects all individuals in business transactions, whether they are dealing with a sole proprietorship, a partnership, a corporation, or any other form of business organization. The key factor is not the form of the business, but whether a legal wrong has been committed.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : REMEDIES FOR A BREACH OF CONTRACT

explanation: Satisfaction (utility) is the most essential element of a product because customer satisfaction is a clear indicator of product success and should be an on going goal not only to keep clients satisfied.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a disadvantage of nationalization
options: The cost of services is not determined by by market forces
It may lead to rationalization of resources
The development of local skills and technology is retarded
explanation: The development of local skills and technology is retarded: Nationalization may impede the development of local skills and technology in several ways. When industries are nationalized, the government often takes control of operations, which can result in a centralized decision-making process that may not prioritize innovation, research, and development. This can lead to a lack of investment in technological advancements and a reduced focus on developing local talent and expertise. Additionally, nationalized industries may face bureaucratic inefficiencies and rigidities that hinder the adoption of new technologies and practices.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PUBLIC CORPORATION : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVATAGES

Trust with shareholders from all West African countries

It has 15 member states: Benin, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, Côte d'Ivoire, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Mali, Mauritania, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, and Sierra Leone.

explanation: Form utility is created by changing the form of raw materials into finished goods e.g production in agriculture or manufacturing creates form utility.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES : FEATURES AND IMPORTANCE

= #139m

Net profit ( before tax) = Gross profit - Overhead expenses
= 139m - 85.5m
= #53.5m

subject: Commerce
q: Kabir receives two quotations. Ade quotes N 100 less 20% trade discount and 20% cash discount while Benson N100 less 30% trade discount and 10% cash discount. If Kabir wants to take advantage and cash discount, then
options: the material should be supplied by Ade
the material should be supplied by both of them
the material should be supplied by Benson
Benson should be ask to reduce his trade discount
explanation:
Ade quotes N 100 less 20% trade discount and 20% cash discount
Amount given out as total discount is
Trade discount = 100 x 20%
= 20
Cash discount = (100 - 20) x20%
= 16
Total discount = 20 + 16
= 36
Kabir pays = 100 - 36
= #64

Benson N100 less 30% trade discount and 10% cash discount
Trade discount = 100 x 30%
= 30
Cash discount = ( 100 - 30) x 10%
= 7
Total discount = 30 + 7
= 37
Kabir pays = 100 - 37
= #63

Kabir should take advantage of Bension offer since the total amount paid and cash discount is lesser than that of Ade

explanation: In a hire-purchase agreement, the seller (Kamaldeen in this case) has the right to terminate the agreement and recover the goods if the buyer (Emeka) fails to meet the terms of the agreement (usually failure to make the required payments). topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: HIRE PURCHASES : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: The main document sent to an importer of goods by the exporter are?
options: invoice, consular invoice, certificate of origin, fright note, indent and insurance policy
indent, bill of lading, certificate of origin, invoice and bill of exchange
certificate of origin, bill of exchange, insurance policy, indent and bill of lading
bill of lading, invoice insurance policy, consular invoice, certificate of origin and bill of exchange
explanation: The main documents sent to an importer of goods by the exporter are the indent, bill of lading, certificate of origin, invoice, and bill of exchange. An indent is a purchase order placed by an importer with an exporter. A bill of lading is a document issued by a carrier to a shipper, acknowledging that specified goods have been received on board as cargo. A certificate of origin is a document declaring in which country a commodity or good was manufactured. An invoice is a commercial document that itemizes a transaction between a buyer and a seller. A bill of exchange is a binding agreement by one party to pay a fixed amount of cash to another party on demand or at a predetermined date.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : DOCMENT USED IN INTERNATION TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: The document that indicate instant payment of cash for goods whenever the are delivered is
explanation: A proforma invoice is a preliminary invoice sent by a seller to a buyer before the actual goods are delivered. It outlines the description of the goods, their prices, and the terms of sale, including payment terms. In many cases, a proforma invoice includes a request for immediate payment, often in advance of the goods being shipped or delivered. It serves as a formal quotation and can be used for various purposes, including customs clearance and as a basis for arranging payment.

topics.title: PURCHASES AND SALES OF GOODS : DOCUMENT USED IN BUYING & SELLING

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following document permit an importer to inspect his goods before the arrival of the bill of lading?
explanation: A Bill of Sight is a document that allows an importer to inspect his goods before the arrival of the bill of lading. It is a temporary document detailing the particulars of the goods that are yet to be inspected in detail. The other options do not serve this purpose. An import invoice is a document provided by the seller to the buyer, detailing the type and value of goods sold. A consular invoice is a document used in foreign trade transactions, certified by the consul of the importing country residing in the foreign country from where the goods are being shipped. A bill of exchange is a written order binding one party to pay a fixed sum of money to another party on demand or at a predetermined date.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : DOCMENT USED IN INTERNATION TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: The overriding advantage of home trade over foreign trade is
explanation: In home trade, the buyer and seller are typically located within the same country or a relatively close geographical region. This physical proximity makes it easier for them to access each other, communicate, and conduct business transactions. In contrast, foreign trade involves dealing with parties in different countries, which can result in greater distances, time zone differences, and cultural and language barriers. These factors can make it more challenging to access and communicate with foreign buyers and sellers. Therefore, the accessibility of the seller to the buyer is a significant advantage of home trade.

Balance Sheet as at 30/3/99

Debtors20 000

explanation: The total fixed capital is calculated by adding the share capital and the reserve fund. From the balance sheet, the share capital is ₦400,000 and the reserve fund is ₦200,000. Therefore, the total fixed capital is ₦400,000 + ₦200,000 = ₦600,000. The closest option to this is ₦575,000.

explanation:

Lack of good transportation network is a fundamental issue because efficient trade relies heavily on robust infrastructure to transport goods and services. Poor transportation infrastructure can lead to higher costs, delays, and inefficiencies which directly hinder trade.

Insecurity of investment is another critical factor. Political instability, corruption and lack of legal protections can deter both domestic and foreign investors, reducing the capital available for trade and development.

subject: Commerce
q: Entrusting the management of a company to the courts to enable the settlement of the company's obligations is an indication of
explanation:
Bankruptcy is a legal process that allows a company to reorganize its finances or to liquidate its assets. When a company is in bankruptcy, the courts appoint a trustee to manage the company's assets and to oversee the settlement of its debts.

The trustee may sell the company's assets to pay off its debts, or the company may be reorganized and allowed to continue operating. The outcome of the bankruptcy process depends on the specific circumstances of the company.

Entrusting the management of a company to the courts to enable the settlement of the company's obligations is an indication that the company is in financial distress and may be headed for bankruptcy.

q:
I. Personal savings
II. Retained earnings
III. Accrued Taxes

Which of the items above constitute internal source of financing for companies?
explanation: Internal sources of finance are funds that come from within the business. Personal savings and retained earnings are examples of internal sources of finance. Personal savings refers to the money that the owner of the business has saved up and decides to invest in the business. Retained earnings are the profits that the company has earned and decided to reinvest in the business rather than distribute to shareholders. Accrued taxes, on the other hand, are not a source of finance but a liability.

explanation: The main factors that determine the value of a stock on the Stock Exchange are the dividend and capital gain. Capital gain is the rise in the value of a capital asset (investment or real estate) that gives it a higher worth than the purchase price. Accountant's definition of capital

Economist's definition of capital
The man-made resources or assets that are used for the production of more wealth. Thus, Economist regards capital as a factor of production.

Layman's definition of capital
The amount of money he has invested in his business i.e. the money used to start up a business.

subject: Commerce
q: The mode of transporting crude oil to the ports for export purposes is by
explanation:
Some of the most popular ways of transporting crude oil and gas across the world:
- Trucks: The easiest and most utilized form of transportation in the oil and gas industry is trucking. With on-the-road transportation, you can schedule shipments at any time and move them at any place. This advantage makes trucking way more beneficial than other transportation modes. Also, it moves smaller amounts of oil, meaning that the extent of damage in case of cracks or leaks is relatively small.

- Pipelines:
This transportation method suggests that oil is gathered and then transported directly to the site or plant via a pipeline network. Despite pipeline shipping being a hot topic of discussions between industry experts, it is a fast and reliable way to distribute oil. New technology helps advance the safety of this mode, and pipelines have sensors for monitoring important indicators like temperature, pressure, etc. it limits areas and destinations you can ship to because the pipeline infrastructure is not developed enough. Additionally, pipelines move enormous amounts of oil, so when an accident happens, the damage is very serious and it's often hard to liquidate the consequences.

- Ocean:
Ships are a slow, inexpensive, reliable way to move extremely large volumes of fuel. Ocean transportation has one big advantage over other shipping methods, which is that you can transport materials globally. Despite longer transit, it is the most efficient mode for moving oil across the world. Essentially, ocean shipping is mostly used as a part of a large intermodal strategy.

- Rail:
Rail transport was a primary way to move oil for more than 150 years. Rail transportation makes oil containers less prone to damage compared to other shipping modes. This method doesn't have enough flexibility to move oil at any point. But, when combined with other modes, rail can be extremely beneficial for a fairly fast and cost-effective way to move oil long-distance.

- Market research: This involves gathering information about customers, competitors, and the market environment.
- Product development: This involves creating products and services that meet the needs of customers.
- Pricing: This involves setting prices that are fair to both customers and the company.
- Distribution: This involves getting products and services to customers.
- Promotion: This involves communicating the value of products and services to customers.

Of all of these activities, market research is the most important because it helps businesses to understand the needs of their customers. By understanding the needs of their customers, businesses can create products and services that meet those needs and are more likely to be successful.

The aims and objectives of trade associations are:

- to maintain professional ethics of their line of trade
- to defend and advance the interest of members
- to assist members who are in need
- to act as pressure groups in order to influence some government policies

Aims and objectives of Chamber of Commerce are:

Ojo purchased the following items from Sani at the specified prices on
credit:
200 cartons of milk at ₦1,000 per carton; 150 bags of sugar at ₦500 per bag; 50 bags of rice at ₦3,000 per bag. Sani allowed 10% trade discount and 5% cash discount, if payment is made within the agreed period.Ojo paid within the agreed period.

What was the amount paid by Ojo?

topics.title: PURCHASES AND SALES OF GOODS : TERMS OF PAYMENT

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the information below to answer the question that follows
Ojo purchased the following items from Sani at the specified prices on
credit:
200 cartons of milk at ₦1,000 per carton; 150 bags of sugar at ₦500 per bag; 50 bags of rice at ₦3,000 per bag. Sani allowed 10% trade discount and 5% cash discount, if payment is made within the agreed period.Ojo paid within the agreed period.
How much was the trade discount?
explanation: The trade discount is calculated as a percentage of the total cost of the items. In this case, the total cost is ₦425,000 (as calculated in the previous question). So, the trade discount is ₦425,000 * 10% = ₦42,500.

subject: Commerce
q: One of the functions of sea ports is the provision of
options: control room for relaying of radio massage
explanation:
Seaports are facilities that allow ships to dock and unload their cargo. They typically have berths, which are areas where ships can dock, and wharves, which are structures that extend into the water to provide a place for ships to load and unload cargo.

Seaports also have other facilities, such as warehouses for storing cargo, cranes for loading and unloading cargo, and roads and railways for transporting cargo to and from the port.

However, the most important function of a seaport is to provide berthing and landing facilities. Without these facilities, ships would not be able to dock and unload their cargo.

Use the information below to answer the question that follows

Find the current ratio
explanation:
Current ratio = current assets ÷ current liability

Current assets = 50000+145000+120000
= 315000

Current liability = 50000+50000+70000
= 170000

= 315000 ÷ 170000
= 1.85 : 1

subject: Commerce
q: The most importance of computer is
explanation:
Computers are used to facilitate exchanges in many ways, including:

- Processing transactions: Computers are used to process transactions such as sales, purchases, and payments. This makes it easier for businesses to track their finances and manage their inventory.
- Storing data: Computers can store large amounts of data, which can be used to track customer information, product inventory, and financial records. This makes it easier for businesses to make decisions and improve their operations.
- Communicating: Computers can be used to communicate with customers, suppliers, and other businesses. This can be done through email, online chat, and video conferencing. This makes it easier for businesses to collaborate and build relationships.
- Providing information: Computers can be used to provide information to customers, employees, and other stakeholders. This can be done through websites, databases, and reports. This makes it easier for businesses to share information and make informed decisions.

explanation: A bank draft is a type of check where the payment is guaranteed to be available by the issuing bank. It is a payment on behalf of a payer that is guaranteed by the issuing bank. Hence, a bank draft is a check that is drawn on a bank's funds and guaranteed by the bank that issues it.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : TYPES OF BANK

subject: Commerce
q: A form of credit offered by both commercial and merchant banks in Nigeria is
explanation:
Overdraft: This facility is a general form of short-term credit provided by banks that allows individuals or businesses to withdraw more money than they have in their accounts, up to a predetermined limit. Overdrafts are very common and widely used by both commercial and merchant banks to offer immediate liquidity to their customers for various purposes.

NOTE:
Discounting Facility: This service is indeed offered by banks, where they purchase bills of exchange or promissory notes from a business at a discount before their maturity date, providing the business with immediate funds. However, discounting is typically more specialized and used in specific business transactions involving receivables.


While both facilities are available, overdraft is more universally applicable to a wider range of customers (both individual and business) and is more frequently utilized for everyday financial needs. Discounting facilities, although important, are more specialized and primarily used in business contexts involving trade receivables and specific financial instruments.

subject: Commerce
q: The excess of the assets over the liabilities of a business is called
explanation:
Assets are the things that a business owns, such as cash, inventory, and property. Liabilities are the things that a business owes, such as accounts payable and debt. Net worth is the difference between assets and liabilities.

Net worth is also known as equity or owner's equity. It represents the amount of money that the owners have invested in the business, plus the profits that the business has generated over time.

topics.title: MONEY : FUNCTIONS OF MONEY

subject: Commerce
q: A good financial instrument which serves as a hedge against inflation is
explanation:
Traditionally, investments such as gold and real estate are preferred as a good hedge against inflation. However, some investors still prefer investing in stocks with the hope of offsetting inflation in the long term.

Specifically, inflation-protected bonds, such as Treasury Inflation-Protected Securities (TIPS) in the United States, are designed to help investors protect their purchasing power from the erosive effects of inflation. These bonds adjust their principal value in line with changes in the consumer price index (CPI), ensuring that the bondholder receives compensation for inflation. So, as inflation rises, the principal amount of the bond increases, providing a hedge against the loss of real value caused by inflation.

Rate of turnovers = Cost of goods sold/Average stock

Cost of goods sold = Opening stock + Purchases - Closing stock
= 5600 + 9700 - 4400
= 10,900

Average Stocks = (Opening stock + Closing stock)/2
= (5,600 + 4,400)/2
= 5,000

Therefore, ROS = 10,900/5,000
= 2.18 times.

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question.

In the communication process, Y represents
explanation:
The communication process are:
1. Source (Sender): The initiator of the communication process.
2. Stimulus: The trigger that sets the communication process in motion.
3. Message: The content or information being communicated.
4. Encoding: The process of putting thoughts, ideas, and information together into a communicable form.
5. Transmission (Medium): The means or medium through which the message is delivered.
6. Noise: Any interference that distorts the message.
7. Receiver: The person who receives the message.
8. Decoding: The process of translating and interpreting the received message.
9. Feedback: The response from the receiver to the sender, indicating whether the message was understood.
Given this arrangement, these components to the diagram:
Source (Sender): This is labeled as "sender" in the diagram.
Stimulus: This might not be explicitly shown in the diagram.
Message: Implicit in the process; the content being communicated.
Encoding: Implicit in the sender's role.
Transmission (Medium): This corresponds to Y in the diagram.
Noise: Not explicitly shown but would affect the transmission (Y).
Receiver: This corresponds to X in the diagram.
Decoding: Implicit in the receiver's role.
Feedback: Explicitly shown as "Feedback" in the diagram.

So, the correct interpretation of Y in the context of the communication process and the provided diagram is that Y represents the medium or means of transmission.

explanation:

q: The main requirement for admission to the second tier securities market is the
options: creation of a good atmosphere for growth and development
explanation:
The Second Tier Securities Market (SSM) is a market established by the Nigerian Stock Exchange (NSE) to provide an alternative listing platform for smaller companies. The SSM has lower listing requirements than the Main Market of the NSE, making it a more accessible option for smaller companies.

One of the main requirements for admission to the SSM is the disclosure of all trading activities. This means that the company must provide the NSE with information about all of its trading activities, including the number of shares traded, the prices at which they were traded, and the identities of the buyers and sellers.

The other requirements for admission to the SSM include:
- The company must be incorporated in Nigeria.
- The company must have a minimum share capital of ₦10 million.
- The company must have a minimum of 100 shareholders.
- The company must have a three-year track record of profitability.

subject: Commerce
q: The factor which critically determines the choice of occupation is
explanation:
Interest is the strongest factor that determines the choice of occupation. When people are interested in something, they are more likely to be motivated to learn about it and to do well in it. They are also more likely to find satisfaction in their work.

Skills and training are also important factors, but they are not as critical as interest. Skills can be learned, and training can be provided. However, it is difficult to learn or train someone to be interested in something.

Aptitude is another factor that can influence the choice of occupation. Aptitude is a natural ability or talent for something. However, aptitude is not always a guarantee of success. People with high aptitude for a particular occupation may not be interested in it, or they may not have the necessary skills or training.

In conclusion, the factor that most critically determines the choice of occupation is interest. However, skills, training, and aptitude can also play a role.

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following describes inputs for any production activity?
explanation:
Natural resources: These are raw materials obtained from nature that are used in production, such as minerals, water, and land.

Business resources: This term can encompass all types of resources (natural, human, and material) used in business activities.

Human resources: This includes the labor, skills, and expertise provided by people that contribute to the production process.

Material resources: These are physical items used in production, such as raw materials and machinery.

In a more focused context, the answer might be more specific:
- If the question is asking for a general term that encompasses all inputs, then Business resources is correct.

- If the question is focusing on the different types of specific inputs, then natural, human and material are all correct in their respective contexts.

Given the context of production activities, all these resources (natural, human, and material) are essential inputs. So, if choosing one encompassing term, Business resources is appropriate.

DASD stands for Direct Access Storage Device. DASDs are used to store a wide variety of data, including operating systems, application software, user data, and databases. They are also used to store backup and recovery data.

q:
Use the information below to answer this question
Balance Sheet of XYZ as at 31/12/2003


₦ ₦
Ordinary share capital 25 000 000 Fixed assets 26 000 000
Revenue reserve 10 000 000 Investment 22 000 000
General reserve 3 000 000 Current assets 5 000 000
Long term liabilities 4 000 000
Current liabilities 11 000 000
53 000 000 53 000 000


What is the net working capital
explanation:
The net working capital = current assets - current liabilities
=5000000 - 11000000
= - 6,000,000
Note that ( ) means Minus (-) in accounting i.e deficit
Therefore the answer is (#6,000,000)

General reserve 3 000 000 Current assets 5 000 000
Long term liabilities 4 000 000
Current liabilities 11 000 000
53 000 000 53 000 000

Determine the owners' equity
explanation:
Owner's equity = ordinary share capital + revenue reserve + general reserve

= ₦38,000,000

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the information below to answer this question
Maimani PLC, whose nominal capital is made up of 5,000,000 ordinary shares of 50k each, decided to offer 80% of it to the public and realised only 50% of the amount due.
Determine the company's reserve share capital
explanation:
Issued capital = 80% of ₦2,500,000 = ₦2,000,000
Subscribed capital = 40% of ₦2,000,000 = ₦1,000,000
Reserve = ₦2,000,000 - ₦1,000,000
= ₦1,000,000

subject: Commerce
q: Balance of payments is made up of
explanation:
Visible items are goods that are traded between countries. Invisible items are services that are traded between countries.

The balance of payments is a statement of all economic transactions between a country and the rest of the world over a period of time. It is divided into two main accounts: the current account and the capital and financial account.

The current account records the trade in goods and services, as well as income and transfers. The capital and financial account records the movement of assets and liabilities between residents of a country and non-residents.

Visible items are recorded in the current account, while invisible items are also recorded in the current account.

subject: Commerce
q: The most commonly used commercial documents are
options: order notes, debit notes, invoices and quotations
letter of inquiry, consignment notes, receipt and invoices
credit notes, statement of accounts, dispatch order and bills
explanation:
These documents are used in a variety of commercial transactions, including sales, purchases, and consignments.

Letter of inquiry: A letter of inquiry is a document that is used to request information about a product or service. It is typically used by businesses that are considering buying or selling goods or services.

Consignment note: A consignment note is a document that is used to track the movement of goods that are being consigned from one party to another. A consignment note typically includes information about the goods being consigned, the parties involved, and the terms of the consignment agreement.

Receipt: A receipt is a document that is used to acknowledge the receipt of goods or services. A receipt typically includes information about the goods or services received, the date of receipt, and the amount paid.

Invoice: An invoice is a document that is used to bill a customer for goods or services that have been provided. An invoice typically includes information about the goods or services being billed, the quantity, the price, and the terms of payment.

Other commonly used commercial documents include:

Order note: A document that is used to place an order for goods or services.
Debit note: A document that is used to issue a credit to a customer's account.
Credit note: A document that is used to issue a debit to a customer's account.
Statement of account: A document that is used to summarize a customer's account activity over a period of time.
Dispatch order: A document that is used to authorize the shipment of goods.
Bill of lading: A document that is used to track the movement of goods and to provide evidence of ownership of goods.

explanation:
The economic environment refers to all the economic factors that affect commercial and consumer behavior. The growth rate is the rate at which the economy is expanding. It is measured as the percentage change in real gross domestic product (GDP) from one year to the next.

The growth rate is an important factor in the economic environment because it affects the demand for goods and services. When the economy is growing, businesses tend to experience higher demand for their products and services. This can lead to increased sales and profits.

q: The three components of a country's balance of payment are
options: capital account, trade, account and business record

monetary movement account, trade account and sales ledger
current account, capital account and monetary movement account
explanation: A country's balance of payment is made up of three components: the current account, the capital account, and the financial account. The current account records a nation's transactions with the rest of the world—specifically its net trade in goods and services, its net earnings on cross-border investments, and its net transfer payments. The capital account records the net change in ownership of foreign assets. The financial account records the net change in ownership of international monetary assets.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : BALANCE OF PAYMENT

subject: Commerce
q: A document that indicates obligation that is transferable by delivery and endorsement is
explanation:
A negotiation instrument is a document that gives the holder the right to demand payment from the maker of the instrument. Negotiation instruments are transferable by delivery and endorsement. This means that the holder of the instrument can transfer their right to payment to another person by delivering the instrument to them and endorsing it.

There are two main types of negotiation instruments: bills of exchange and promissory notes.
- A bill of exchange is a written order from one person (the drawer) to another person (the drawee) to pay a third person (the payee) a certain sum of money on demand or at a future date.
- A promissory note is a written promise from one person (the maker) to pay a certain sum of money to another person (the payee) on demand or at a future date.

Both bills of exchange and promissory notes can be used as negotiation instruments. When a negotiation instrument is transferred by delivery and endorsement, the endorser becomes liable for the payment of the instrument to the holder.

require a modem before it could be used
explanation:
Intranet: A private network based on the internet protocol for sharing information, collaboration tools, operational systems and other computing services within an organization, usually to the exclusion of access by outsiders. It is restricted to employees of the organization.

Extranet: A controlled private network that allows access to partners, vendors and supplier or an authorized set of customers. It is for information sharing and restricted to those users who have login credentials.

explanation: A web browser is a software application that allows a user to display and interact with text, images, and other information typically located on a web page at a website on the World Wide Web or a local area network. Internet protocol is a set of rules governing the format of data sent over the Internet or other network. Corel Draw is a vector graphics editor, not a software for interacting with text, images, and videos. A web server is a computer system that processes requests via HTTP, the basic network protocol used to distribute information on the World Wide Web.

HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) is the protocol used for transmitting hypertext via the internet. TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) is one of the main protocols in IP networks, while IP (Internet Protocol) is the principal communications protocol in the Internet protocol suite for relaying datagrams across network boundaries.

The other options are incorrect as the @ symbol does not link the user with other internet users, locate the addresses of internet users, or link the user with the machine.

explanation: Communication is relevant to business activities because it enhances the delivery of goods and services. While communication can connect people and potentially reduce the cost and risk of travelling, these are not its primary relevance to business activities. explanation: Tourism serves the purpose of cross-cultural understanding and peaceful interaction. explanation:
A cooperative is a business organization that exploits the capabilities of a member to remedy the weakness of another. In a cooperative, each member contributes resources and shares in the control of the organization on an equal basis.

A joint venture is a business arrangement in which two or more parties agree to pool their resources for the purpose of accomplishing a specific task.

A partnership is a business owned by two or more people who share its profits and losses.

A nominal partnership is one in which a person lends his or her name to a firm but does not actually participate in its management or share in its profits.

explanation: Long-term financing refers to the type of financing that is provided for a period of more than a year. A business organization can obtain long-term financing through the sale of shares. Paid-up capital is the amount of money a company has received from shareholders in exchange for shares. Reserved capital is a part of the profits of a company that is set aside to strengthen the company's financial position.

explanation:
Rate = 4 times in 1999
Turnover = amount × rate
= ₦49,600 × 4

= ₦198,400.

topics.title: FINANCING BUSINESS : TURNOVER

subject: Commerce
q:
Given
Opening Stock₦
1 800
Purchases₦
2 800
Sales ₦8 000
Closing Stock₦
350
Carriage on Sales ₦500

Calculate the value of unused stock
explanation: The value of unused stock is calculated by adding the opening stock and purchases, then subtracting the sales and carriage on sales. In this case, the opening stock is ₦1,800, purchases are ₦2,800, sales are ₦8,000, and carriage on sales is ₦500. Therefore, the value of unused stock is (₦1,800 + ₦2,800) - (₦8,000 + ₦500) = ₦350. The correct option is ₦350.

subject: Commerce
q:
Yayha bought a piece of furniture on a credit sale agreement from Ahmed and resold it to Ali before all instalments were made. The court upheld that
Ahmed could not recover possession of the items from Ali. The reason for the judgement was because
options: ownership was transferred on completion of instalment
Yahya could not pass title to a third party
explanation: In a credit sale agreement, the ownership of the goods is transferred to the buyer upon delivery, not upon completion of payment. Therefore, Yahya was able to legally sell the furniture to Ali, and Ahmed could not recover possession from Ali. This is based on the principle of nemo dat quod non habet, which means 'no one gives what he doesn't have'. Once the furniture was delivered to Yahya, he became the owner and could transfer ownership to Ali.

The organization was established to checkmate illicit and counterfeit products in Nigeria in 1993 under the country's health and safety law. Adulterated and counterfeit drugs are a problem in Nigeria.

explanation: The Construction industry is mostly the assembling of products of the manufacturing industry as products are made and brought together to create a something new.

explanation: Uberrima fides (sometimes seen in its genitive form uberrimae fidei) is a Latin phrase meaning "utmost good faith" (literally, "most abundant faith"). This means that all parties to an insurance contract must deal in good faith, making a full declaration of all material facts in the insurance proposal.

explanation: Money is anything which is backed up by law, generally acceptable as a means of exchange and in the settlement of debt. Legal tender is a form of money that courts of law are required to recognize as satisfactory payment for any monetary debt.

topics.title: MONEY : QUALITIES OF MONEY

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a quality of money?
explanation:
Scarcity is a fundamental quality of money. Money is valuable because it is relatively scarce compared to the demand for it. If money were too abundant, it would lose its value as a medium of exchange and store of value. Money needs to be relatively scarce to maintain its worth and function effectively in an economy. The other qualities mentioned, like convertibility and availability, can also be important aspects of money, but scarcity is a foundational attribute.

The qualities, features or characteristics of money are:
- general acceptability
- recognizability
- homogeneity
- scarcity
- portability
- durability

topics.title: MONEY : QUALITIES OF MONEY

subject: Commerce
q: Money can simply be referred to as a
explanation: Money can be defined as anything that is backed up, generally acceptability as a medium of exchange, and in the settlement of debt. The main functions of money are distinguished as: a medium of exchange; a unit of account; a store of value, standard of deferred payment and as a measure of value.

explanation: Gilt-edged securities are high-grade bonds that issued by national governments and private organizations in order to generate revenue.

explanation:
Core benefit proposition is that proposition put forward by a company which talks about the main and the most important benefit that a consumer would derive from consuming that product. A core proposition can talk about the product in itself and also how it is different from its competitors.

The core benefit of a product is also used in positioning a brand. The core benefit proposition helps customers make an informed buying choice. It is not always that a consumer just looks at the basic benefits that he/she will derive from consuming a product.

q: Cum div differ from ex div in that, the latter
options: confirms a purchase or sale made on behalf of a shareholder
is a document used to transfer ownership of shares
entitles the purchaser to receive a company's current dividend
entitles the vendor to receive a company's current dividend
explanation: Cum div and ex div are terms used in the stock market. Cum div means 'with dividend'. If a stock is bought cum div, the buyer is entitled to receive the next dividend payment. Ex div, on the other hand, means 'without dividend'. If a stock is bought ex div, the seller retains the right to the next dividend payment. Therefore, the statement that ex div 'entitles the purchaser to receive a company's current dividend' is incorrect. The correct statement is that ex div 'entitles the vendor to receive a company's current dividend'.

explanation: Selling creates 'possession utility' which is the value or benefit a customer receives by owning the product. It involves transferring the ownership of a product from the seller to the buyer. The other options, form utility, place utility, and marginal utility, are not specifically created by selling.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : THE MARKETING CONCEPT

explanation: The marketing concept is the philosophy that firms should analyze the needs of their customers and then make decisions to satisfy those needs, better than the competition. Today most firms have adopted the marketing concept, but this has not always been the case.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : THE MARKETING CONCEPT

subject: Commerce
q: The physical and psychological satisfaction a customer derives from the purchase of goods and services is
explanation: The product mix refers to the combination of products and services that a company offers to its customers. It includes the various products or services available for purchase, and it's an essential component of marketing because it directly impacts the satisfaction and value that customers derive from their purchases. The physical and psychological satisfaction that customers experience is a result of the products and services they choose from the product mix.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following aspects of marketing stimulates buying by providing free gifts?
explanation: Sales promotion is a link between personal selling and advertising that stimulate consumer purchase. It includes trade fair, competition, free sample (bearing the firm's logo), premium offer (offer a low value item as a bonus e.g. tooth brush, biro in tooth paste)

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is used as a pricing policy?
explanation: Market Skimming is used as a pricing policy. Others include: Market penetration, product-line pricing, target-return pricing, Bid-pricing, Variable pricing etc.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

explanation: Frustration of contracts refers to a particular way in which contractual obligations can be discharged. A contract is frustrated where further performance of the contract becomes impossible due to an unforeseen event, or series of events, taking place through no fault of the parties to the agreement.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : MEANING AND TYPES OF CONTRACT

explanation:
MERITS OF HIRE PURCHASE

- Buyers enjoy the use of goods even when he has not fully paid for
- There will be an increase in turnover

options: is not in possession of the goods
is licensed to sell goods on auction
has a lien on the goods he possesses
explanation: The difference between a factor and a broker is that the former 'has a lien on the goods he possesses'. A factor is a type of agent who is entrusted with the possession of goods to be sold in his own name as if he were the owner. He has a right of lien on the goods for his dues from the principal on account of goods sold or for any expenses incurred by him for such goods.

explanation: A contract that is devoid of legal effect is a void contract. A void contract is a formal agreement that is illegitimate and unenforceable from the moment it is created. It lacks the necessary elements of a properly constituted contract, hence it has no legal effect.

explanation: There are some functional units of the computer that constitute in producing logical outputs. Five basic functional units of a computer are i.e. input unit, a processing unit (Arithmetic and logic), a memory to store final or intermediate results, a controlling unit that coordinates functions of every unit, and finally an output unit.

topics.title: INFORMATIONAND COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY (ICT) : TYPES OF COMPUTER AND FUNCTIONS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following computers can be used in weather forecast?
explanation: Digital computers are widely employed in weather forecasting and meteorology. These computers can process vast amounts of data, perform complex calculations, and run sophisticated numerical models to simulate weather patterns and make predictions. They are essential for handling the massive data sets involved in modern weather forecasting and providing accurate and timely weather forecasts. While hybrid computers can also be used in certain scientific applications, digital computers are the primary choice for weather forecasting.

topics.title: INFORMATIONAND COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY (ICT) : TYPES OF COMPUTER AND FUNCTIONS

explanation: A Digital Versatile Disk (DVD) is a type of optical disk technology similar to the CD-ROM. It is a computer storage device that can be used to store data, video, audio and other forms of digital information. Therefore, it is not a file transfer protocol, transmission control protocol or a microprocessor.

topics.title: INFORMATIONAND COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY (ICT) : TYPES OF COMPUTER AND FUNCTIONS

Meeting the needs and demands of the shareholders
Contributing to the sustenance and development of its host community
Operating without disrupting the very essence of the environment
explanation: Social responsibility is an important vehicle for building a strong organizational reputation. Organizations with distinctive ethical values and elaborate welfare projects are able to compete effectively in the market. Consumers tend to pledge loyalty to organizations that maintain integrity, good governance and best practice in their operations.

topics.title: BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT AND SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY : FACTORS

explanation: When a lawyer engages in fraudulent activities, the NBA derobes him/her to protect the integrity of the association.

explanation: Copyright is an exclusive or sole sight granted to writers of literary works (authors), musicians and artists to produce his work for a specified period of time. Another person cannot reproduce such work without a written permission from the copyright owner. Literary copyright will last for 50 years after the death of the owner while musical right will only last for 50 years after its release.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: GOVERNMENT REGULATION : CONSUMER PROTECTION

subject: Commerce
q: A country is said to be experiencing an unfavourable balance of trade if her
explanation: Unfavourable balance of trade is used or is said to occur when visible import exceed visible exports. It implies that the country is importing more than it is exporting. A typical example is Nigeria experiencing an unfavourable balance of trade since her imports is greater than her exports. Some of the implications of this include devaluation of currency, overdependence on foreign countries among others.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : BALANCE OF PAYMENT

explanation: A wonderful world is a form of persuasive advertising. It is a type of advertising which tries to persuade customers to buy a particular brand of a product as being different from other brands. Persuasive advertising, which is emotional in nature, is used to induce or arouse customers' interest in order to persuade them to buy a particular product.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: ADVERTISING : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: A major factor that affects business operations is
explanation: Business operations are mostly affected by availability of technology. Technology helps to speed up the rate of production and thus increases sales. Technology facilitates business activities by promoting efficiency and effectiveness of a firm.

subject: Commerce
q: A partner who only make capital contribution but does not take part in the management of the partnership business is a/an
explanation: A limited partner is a partner who contributes capital to a partnership but does not have management authority or unlimited liability. This means they are not involved in the day-to-day operations of the business and their liability is limited to the extent of their investment in the business.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PARTNERSHIP : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

explanation: Money can be referred to as a measure of value. It is a unit of account that provides a consistent way of measuring the value of goods and services. It allows us to compare the value of different goods and services and make economic decisions.

topics.title: MONEY : MEANING AND TYPES OF MONEY
