explanation: Hardware refers to the physical aspect of computer, telecommunication and other devices. This includes the computer case, monitor, keyboard and mouse. It also includes all the parts inside the computer case as hard disk, video card etc.

topics.title: INFORMATIONAND COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY (ICT) : TYPES OF COMPUTER AND FUNCTIONS

subject: Commerce
q: Buying, selling and distribution of goods and services are components of
explanation: Commerce involves the buying, selling and distribution of goods and services. It is a broader term that encompasses retail trade (selling directly to the end consumer), wholesale trade (selling in large quantities to retailers), and marketing (promoting and selling products or services). Therefore, the correct answer is commerce.

topics.title: TRADE (1) : HOME TRADE: WHOLESALE TRADE

q: A company has an authorized share capital of ₦15,000. It offered applicants ₦10,000 share capital, out of which the company collected ₦7,500. What is the paid-up capital?
explanation: Paid-up capital: This is part of the called up capital which the shareholders have actually paid for. It is the total amount paid up or credited as paid up on the issued share capital. The paid-up capital is ₦7,500.

explanation: Direct advertising is aimed at providing information to a section, target market segment or group of people with relatively similar needs. An advertisement on Jeans for instance, must, as a matter of professionalism be directed to the youths.

control of the flow of goods in and out of the country
explanation: Customs and Excise authority is a department in the Ministry of Internal Affairs charged with the responsibility of assessing and collecting custom duties on import, export and goods produced locally. Hence, customs authority helps to control the flow of goods in and out of the country.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : CUSTOMS, EXCISE, PORTS AUTHORITY ETC.

subject: Commerce
q: The rate of turnover of a firm in a given year is 5 times while the average stock is ₦12,500. What is the turnover of the firm?
explanation:
Turnover or sales of a business is the total net sales during a particular period e.g a year. Rate of turnover is the number of times the value of average stock of a business is sold during a period. Rate of turnover can be computed as:
Cost of goods sold
average stock

Average stock = Opening stock + closing stock ÷ 2
Turnover = Average stock × Rate of Turnover
= 12,500 × 5
= ₦62,500

topics.title: FINANCING BUSINESS : TURNOVER

subject: Commerce
q: Commercial activities in Nigeria have greatly been enhanced due to improvement in
explanation: Transportation involves the movement of goods and people from one place to another. Transportation is one of the ancillaries to trade that helps to facilitate trade. Transport has helped to boost commercial activities in our modern world especially through the advent of cars, aeroplanes among others. It has helped to create place utility.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : MEANING AND TYPES OF TRANSPORTATION

explanation: Non-indemnity insurance refers to those associated risks for which no amount of compensation could equate to the loss suffered by the insured. However, only a consolation payment is made to the insured. An example is Life Assurance.

explanation: A departmental store is a collection of shops under one roof with ownership of each shop or department specialising in selling a special range of goods. Each department normally buys separately, exercise its own stock control and sets its own product policy. It is also a large retailing unit handling a wide variety shopping and specialty goods but organised into separate departments for the purpose of promotion, service and control. There is high degree of departmentalisation e.g Kingsway stores and Leventis stores where each department specialise on one line of product e.g electronics, clothing, sport, equipment etc.

topics.title: TRADE (1) : HOME TRADE: RETAIL TRADE

explanation: One of the services rendered by NITEL is Business Reply Service (BRS). BRS is a service provided by the post office to enable firms wishing to obtain replies from clients without putting the customers at the expense of paying postage fees. The firm would have supplied their self-addressed envelopes or cards which will be used for reply. The firm acquires a license after paying an annual fee to cover the cost of postage which is based on the number of replies received.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: COMMUNICATION : TYPES

explanation: The two main forms of communication are verbal and non-verbal. Verbal communication involves the use of words in delivering the intended message. On the other hand, non-verbal communication involves the use of visual cues like body language, facial expressions, and gestures. The other options like e-mail and fax, traditional and modern media, and oral and written are types or modes of communication, not forms

explanation: When data is transferred one computer to another, this is called Downloading. The recipient computer receives the data from the former and the data is transferred.

subject: Commerce
q: When a cheque is crossed, it holds that
options: the culprit is traceable in the event of theft
it can be cashed over the counter
creditors without current accounts can be paid
explanation: Crossed cheque is one having two parallel lines drawn across its face. It cannot be cashed at the counter; it must be paid into a current account. Some words may be written between the parallel lines e.g "& co" or "Non-negotiable"etc. This helps to prevent fraudulent activities/practices. Forms of crossing could be general and special crossing. General crossing is one in which two parallel transverse lines are drawn across the face of the cheque with or without the addition of the words "& co or "Non-negotiable" In special crossing the name of a bank is inserted between the transverse lines. However, when a cheque is crossed, it holds. "It helps in tracing the culprit in case the cheque was stolen".

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES & CHALLEGES

explanation: Consumerism is an organised effort or action of consumers or individuals to protect themselves against the unfair practices of businessmen. Consumerism came into existence when a movement started in the 1960s to obtain a greater say in the quality of the products they bought and the information they received from sellers. They also sought to increase their influence power and rights.

explanation: Retailing is the final link in the chain of distribution. It involves the sale of goods and services from businesses to end consumers for use. explanation: Market segmentation is the process of dividing a total market into market groups, consisting of people who have relatively similar product need. explanation: A contract can be discharged when the subject matter or basis of the contract has been destroyed or frustrated by an act of God. e.g Flood, war etc or there has been a change in law as in the case of Taylor vs Caldwell. explanation: When an organization issues a cheque to cover the salaries of its employees, it is using a credit transfer system of payment. production of goods and services that satisfy man needs
transfer of title of ownership of goods and services to individuals
explanation: Management of business involves the production of goods and services that satisfy mans needs. Production is not complete until when goods and services reach the final consumers.

explanation: The trading position of a country is the same as its balance of trade. The balance of trade is the difference between the value of a country's exports and the value of its imports. explanation: Gilt-edged security is a security issued by the government; it has a fixed rate of interest. This type of security is considered to be very safe as government cannot fail to pay its debts. Gilt edged carries a minimum issued risk in terms of repayment.

explanation: By scarcity, we mean it must not be too much so as not to lose its value. Also, money must not be in excess in a country in order to avoid the possibility of inflation which has been defined as too much money chasing too few goods.

explanation: The location of cements industry will be invariably influenced by the availability of natural endowment. explanation: Planning involves setting goals and devising specific activities, procedures and schedules for meeting the goals or objectives.

explanation: Unskilled labour is that labour that requires little or no formal education. explanation: A microprocessor controls all functions of the CPU, or central processing unit, of a computer or other digital device. The microprocessor functions as an artificial brain. The entire function of the CPU is controlled by a single integrated circuit.

explanation: Market segmentation is the process of dividing a total market into market groups, consisting of people who have relatively similar product need. The aim of segmentation is that it enables the sellers to make appropriate set of product mix, quality price, advertisement etc.

explanation: The terms of trade are favourable of the average price of exports is higher than the average price of imports. Exports become relatively more expensive than imports. If the prices of export rise in relation to the prices of imports the terms of trade will improve, since a given quantity of exports will pay for more imports. Favourable Terms of Trade leads to a rise in real national income.

explanation: Money is anything that is generally accepted as a medium of exchange for making payments, settlement of debts and other business obligations. The government of a country places a legal bucking on money which makes it to be generally acceptable.

explanation: By Print Advertising, we mean, advertising done through Newspaper, Magazines, fliers etc which only allows the literate in the society to be the only ones eligible to purchase it.

explanation: The motor dealer has breached the contract. A breach of contract is when one party fails to fulfill their obligations under the contract. explanation: Industrial waste constitute a major source of pollution in Nigeria. This is due to the use of generator and high powered diesels which emits their pollution into the air.

explanation: Manufacturing industry involves the process of transformation of raw materials into finished or semi-finished products. Rice processing can be classified under manufacturing activity. Other examples include Beer making, Bread making etc.

explanation: Communication is the means of sending and receiving information from one place to another. delivery → Cash payment.

explanation: While the unit of account is the precise term for the general acceptability of money as a standard of value, the medium of exchange is a function that inherently relies on the acceptability of money. Turnover or sales refers to the total value of net sales of a business during a particular period. Rate of turnover on the other hand is the number of times the average stock is turned over. The rate of turnover is computed thus:
R.O.T=cost of good sold
average stock

whereAverage stock=closing stock + Opening stock
2

Turnover of firm = Average stock × rate of turnover
= ₦12,500 × 5
= ₦62,500

This describes the doctrine of
explanation: The phrase 'the consumer is always right' describes the doctrine of 'consumer sovereignty'. The other options do not accurately represent this concept.

explanation: The retailer typically operates from a stall or a shop, while the wholesaler operates from a warehouse. A warehouse is a large building where goods are stored before being distributed to retailers or customers.

explanation: The main disadvantage of water transport is that it is generally slow compared to other modes of transport, such as air or road transport. While it can carry heavy goods and travel long distances, the speed factor is a significant drawback.

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following requires a certificate of trading to start business?
explanation: A Public Limited Company requires a certificate of trading to start business. This is because a Public Limited Company is a type of business that offers its securities (stock/shares, bonds/loans, etc.) for sale to the general public, typically through a stock exchange. The certificate of trading is a legal document that confirms that the company is legally allowed to start trading. This is different from a Private Limited Company, Partnership, or Cooperative Society, which do not require a certificate of trading to start business.

explanation: A Trade Association is an organization founded and funded by businesses that operate in a specific industry. They may offer other services, such as producing conferences, networking or charitable events or offering classes or educational materials.

explanation: Retailers are businesses or individuals that sell goods directly to the end user or consumer. While some retailers may operate as sole proprietors or sell on hire purchase, not all do. Therefore, the only feature common to all retailers is that they sell to the final consumers.

explanation: A consignment note is a document that accompanies goods being transported by a common carrier and describes the goods, the details of the consignor and consignee. The other options are not specifically used for goods being sent by a common carrier.

explanation: The combination of product, price, promotion, and distribution decisions employed by a company is referred to as its marketing mix. The marketing mix is often crucial when determining a product or brand's offer, and is often associated with the four P's: price, product, promotion, and place.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

explanation: The bill of lading is a document issued by a carrier to acknowledge receipt of cargo for shipment. It details the type, quantity, and destination of the goods being carried, which is crucial for assessing customs duty.

subject: Commerce
q: An association of businessmen engaged in both commerce and industry is called
explanation: A Chamber of Commerce is a form of business network, e.g., a local organization of businesses whose goal is to further the interests of businesses. Business owners in towns and cities form these local societies to advocate on behalf of the business community. They are not a trade union (which is an organization of workers who have come together to achieve common goals such as protecting the integrity of its trade, improving safety standards, etc.), a trade association (which is an organization founded and funded by businesses that operate in a specific industry), or a trust (which is a fiduciary relationship in which one party, known as a trustor, gives another party, the trustee, the right to hold title to property or assets for the benefit of a third party).

topics.title: TRADE ASSOCIATION : CHAMBER OF COMMERCE

explanation: A trade association is an organization founded and funded by businesses that operate in a specific industry. These associations aim to collaborate on industry-specific issues, advocate for policies favorable to their industry, and provide a platform for networking, sharing best practices, and addressing common challenges. This distinguishes them from trade unions, which represent workers' rights; chambers of commerce, which serve the broader business community; and cooperative societies, which are owned and operated by members for their mutual benefit.

explanation: Goods bought into a country for sale are referred to as visible imports. This is because they are tangible goods that can be seen and touched, and they are being imported into the country, not exported.

Guinea joined in 1980 and Côte d'Ivoire in 2008. The MRU is named for the Mano River which begins in the Guinea highlands and forms a border between Liberia and Sierra Leone.

explanation: An entrepreneur is someone who starts and runs their own business, taking on financial risks in the hope of profit. A sole proprietor is a type of entrepreneur who owns and operates a business individually. q: Which of the following is a document of title to goods?
explanation: A document of title to goods is a document that provides evidence of a person's entitlement to the possession of goods. The Bill of Lading is a document issued by a carrier (or their agent) to acknowledge receipt of cargo for shipment. It serves three purposes: it is a receipt for the goods shipped, evidence of the contract of carriage, and a document of title to the goods.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : DOCUMENT USED IN INTERNATIONAL TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Divesting government interest in a business to the public is
explanation: Divesting government interest in a business to the public is known as privatization. This is when the government sells off its ownership or interest in a state-owned enterprise to private owners. Nationalization is the opposite of privatization, it is when a government takes control of a private industry or assets. Deregulation is the reduction or elimination of government power in a particular industry, usually enacted to create more competition within the industry. Commercialization is the process of bringing a new product or service to the market.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PUBLIC CORPORATION : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is not a right of the consumer?
explanation: The right to discount is not a recognized right of the consumer. The recognized consumer rights include the right to safety, the right to be informed, the right to choose, and the right to be heard.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: GOVERNMENT REGULATION : CONSUMER PROTECTION

subject: Commerce
q: A dormant partner is one who
options: does not partake in running the business
explanation: A dormant partner is one who does not partake in running the business. They are partners who invest capital into the business but do not participate in its day-to-day operations or management. Their liability is not limited, and they do not necessarily lend their name to the business.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PARTNERSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: Who among the following comes first in the channel of distribution?
explanation: In the channel of distribution, the manufacturer comes first. The manufacturer is the one who produces the goods. After production, the goods are then passed on to the wholesaler, then to the retailer, and finally to the consumer. So, the correct answer is Manufacturer.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

subject: Commerce
q: Insurance is necessary because of the need to
explanation: Insurance is necessary because it provides a means to manage risks and protect against potential future losses or uncertainties. It is a tool that allows individuals or businesses to replace the uncertainty of a potential significant financial loss with a known smaller payment (the insurance premium).

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : PRINCIPLES

subject: Commerce
q: The worth of a life assurance policy before its maturity date is
explanation: The worth of a life assurance policy before its maturity date is known as the surrender value. This is the amount the policyholder will receive from the life insurance company if they choose to exit the policy before its maturity date. The surrender value is usually a percentage of the total premiums paid.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : TERMS

subject: Commerce
q: A house insured against damage by flood was burnt down and the owner had no claim. Which of the following principles prevented him from making a claim?
explanation: The principle that prevented the owner from making a claim is 'Proximate cause'. The principle of proximate cause states that the loss must be directly caused by the peril insured against. In this case, the house was insured against damage by flood, but it was burnt down. Since the cause of the loss (fire) is not the peril insured against (flood), the owner cannot make a claim.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : PRINCIPLES

subject: Commerce
q: The par value of a share is also known as its
explanation: The par value of a share is also known as its nominal price. The par value is the face value of a bond or stock as stated by the issuer. It is the value at which the share was originally issued and it remains constant over time, regardless of the market price of the share.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE : TYPES OF SECURITIES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is not the duty of an entrepreneur?
options: decision on the amount of tax pay
explanation: An entrepreneur is responsible for the provision of initial capital, bearing of risks, and organization of other factors of production. However, the decision on the amount of tax to pay is not the duty of an entrepreneur. This is usually determined by the government's tax regulations and policies.

topics.title: PRODUCTION : FACTORS OF PRODUCTION

subject: Commerce
q: Any activity aimed at finding out the wants of consumers and applying them at a profit is referred to as
explanation: The activity described in the question is marketing. Marketing involves identifying the needs and wants of consumers and finding ways to meet these needs and wants in a profitable manner. Promotion, segmentation, and penetration are all components or strategies of marketing, but they do not encompass the entire process.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MEANING AND IMPORTANCE AND FUNCTIONS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following types of banks primarily deals with the general public and offers services such as savings and checking accounts?
explanation: Commercial banks are financial institutions that provide services to the general public, including accepting deposits, providing checking accounts, and offering loans. They are the most common type of bank that individuals and businesses interact with.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : TYPES OF BANK

subject: Commerce
q: The following is true about a Central Bank except:
options: It acts as a banker to the government
explanation: Central Banks do not provide loans to individuals; instead, they focus on regulating the banking system, managing the country's currency and monetary policy, and acting as a banker to the government. Loans to individuals are typically provided by commercial banks.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES AND CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: Equity interest in a company is represented by
explanation: Equity interest in a company is represented by ordinary shares. Ordinary shares give shareholders ownership in the company and the right to vote at shareholder meetings, as well as the potential to receive dividends.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: Where two or more companies agree to work on a project too large for one of them, this is called
explanation: When two or more companies agree to work together on a project that is too large for one company to handle alone, this arrangement is known as a 'consortium'. A consortium allows companies to pool resources and expertise to achieve a common goal.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: MERGER : MEANING AND TYPES OF MERGER

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the following information to answer the question

Fatima trading company' stock as at 1st January 2017 was 32,000 and as at 31st December 2017, was #4,000. She purchased goods worth #6,000 during the trading year and paid #1,000 as rent.

The average stock was
explanation:
The average stock can be calculated using the formula: (Opening Stock + Closing Stock) / 2. Here, the opening stock is #32,000 and the closing stock is #4,000.

Thus, the average stock = (#32,000 + #4,000) / 2

= #18,000.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : PROFIT

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the following information to answer the question

Fatima trading company' stock as at 1st January 2017 was 32,000 and as at 31st December 2017, was #4,000. She purchased goods worth #6,000 during the trading year and paid #1,000 as rent.

The cost of goods sold is
explanation:
To calculate the cost of goods sold (COGS), we use the formula: COGS = Opening Stock + Purchases - Closing Stock.

Here, COGS = #32,000 + #6,000 - #4,000
= #34,000.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : PROFIT

subject: Commerce
q: A percentage reduction from the quoted-price of goods given to a buyer to encourage bulk purchase is called
explanation: A trade discount is a reduction in price given by the wholesaler/manufacturer to the retailer at the list price or catalogue price. This is done to encourage the retailer to buy more quantities. Therefore, a percentage reduction from the quoted-price of goods given to a buyer to encourage bulk purchase is called a trade discount.

topics.title: PURCHASES AND SALES OF GOODS : TERMS OF TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: The mode of transportation that conveys a limited variety of products through fixed laid routes is
explanation: The correct answer is 'rail'. Rail transportation is characterized by fixed routes and is capable of conveying a limited variety of products, making it suitable for bulk goods and heavy items. Unlike road transport, which is more flexible, rail transport operates on established tracks.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : MEANING AND TYPES OF TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: A requirement for an offer to be valid is that it should be
explanation: For an offer to be valid, it must be communicated to the offeree. The offeree must know about the offer in order to accept it. Therefore, a requirement for an offer to be valid is that it should be communicated to the offeree.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : ELEMENT OF A VALID CONTRACT

subject: Commerce
q: A partnership agreement specifying the relationships amongst partners is called
explanation: A partnership agreement specifying the relationships amongst partners is called a 'Partnership Deed'. This document outlines the terms and conditions of the partnership, including the roles, responsibilities, and profit-sharing arrangements among partners.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PARTNERSHIP : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: The relationship between a country's total receipts and payments in international trade in a given year is called
explanation: The relationship between a country's total receipts and payments in international trade in a given year is referred to as the 'Balance of payments'. This includes all transactions made between entities in one country and the rest of the world, encompassing trade in goods and services, as well as financial transfers.

topics.title: TRADE : FOREIGN TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: RRF as a scheme introduced by the Central Bank of Nigeria means
explanation: RRF stands for 'Refinancing and Rediscounting Facility', which is a scheme introduced by the Central Bank of Nigeria to provide liquidity to banks. This facility allows banks to refinance their loans and rediscount eligible financial instruments. The correct option that matches this answer is 'Refinancing and Rediscounting Facility'.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES AND CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: A document used as a polite way of refusing credit is
explanation: A proforma invoice is a document that is sent to a buyer in certain circumstances, usually before all details of the sale are known. It provides a detailed breakdown of what the seller intends to charge the buyer, but it is not an official demand for payment. It is often used as a polite way of refusing credit to a customer.

topics.title: PURCHASES AND SALES OF GOODS : DOCUMENT USED IN BUYING & SELLING

subject: Commerce
q: An employer insure against his employees's dishonesty by taking up
explanation: An employer can insure against employees' dishonesty through a Fidelity Guarantee Insurance policy. This type of insurance protects businesses from losses caused by dishonest acts of employees, such as theft or fraud. The correct option is 'Fidelity guarantee insurance', which specifically addresses this concern.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: The liquid assets of commercial banks are
options: treasury bills, bills of exchange and cash
cash, current account and other money bearing assets
explanation: Liquid assets are assets that can be converted into cash quickly and with minimal impact to the price received. They are called liquid because they can be 'liquidated' (i.e., sold) quickly. For commercial banks, these include treasury bills, bills of exchange, and cash. Treasury bills are short-term securities issued by a government to finance its short-term requirements. Bills of exchange are a kind of check or promissory note without interest, used primarily in international trade. Cash is the most liquid asset.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES & CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: In which of the following do shareholders vote according to their shareholding?
explanation: In a 'Public limited company', shareholders vote according to their shareholding. This means that the number of votes a shareholder has is proportional to the number of shares they own, allowing them to influence decisions based on their investment.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PUBLIC CORPORATION : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: The working capital of a business is calculated as
explanation: The working capital of a business is calculated as 'Current assets minus current liabilities'. This measure indicates the short-term financial health of a company and its ability to cover its short-term obligations with its short-term assets.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : TYPES OF CAPITAL

subject: Commerce
q: For over staying in a port, a ship is charged a liner called
explanation: Demurrage is a charge that a ship pays for overstaying its allotted time at a port. It is a penalty for exceeding the stipulated laytime (the time agreed between the parties for loading or unloading without payment additional to the freight).

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : DOCUMENT USED IN TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the balance sheet below to answer the question

POLAC Block Company's Balance sheets As At 31/12/2015
# #
Share capital 20000 fixed assets 18000
Reserves 10000 investment 10000
Long term liabilities 4000 current assets 5000
Current liabilities 3000 fictitious assets 4000
37000 37000


The total sum of financing short term project is
explanation: To determine the total sum of financing for short-term projects, we need to look at the current liabilities and current assets. In the balance sheet provided, the current liabilities are 3000 and the current assets are 5000. The financing for short-term projects typically comes from current liabilities, which represent the obligations that need to be settled in the short term. Therefore, the total sum of financing for short-term projects is 5000, which corresponds to the current assets available to cover these liabilities.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : SOURCE OF FINANCE

subject: Commerce
q:
Use the balance sheet below to answer the question

POLAC Block Company's Balance sheets As At 31/12/2015
# #
Share capital 20000 fixed assets 18000
Reserves 10000 investment 10000
Long term liabilities 4000 current assets 5000
Current liabilities 3000 fictitious assets 4000
37000 37000



What is the capital employed?
explanation:
Capital employed is calculated as total assets minus current liabilities.

In this case, total assets are 37000, and current liabilities are 3000, leading to a capital employed of 37000 - 3000
= #34000.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : TYPES OF CAPITAL

subject: Commerce
q: An agreement whereby the seller transfers the title to goods at a price is
explanation: An agreement whereby the seller transfers the title to goods at a price is known as a 'Contract of Sale'. This legal agreement outlines the terms under which the seller agrees to transfer ownership of goods to the buyer in exchange for payment.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : MEANING AND TYPES OF CONTRACT

subject: Commerce
q: Dissolution of a partnership could arise from
explanation: Dissolution of a partnership could arise from the death of a member. A partnership is a business arrangement where two or more individuals share the ownership, profits, and liabilities of a business venture. When one of the partners dies, the partnership is usually dissolved unless there is an agreement in place that allows the partnership to continue.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PARTNERSHIP : DISSOLUTION AND LIQUIDATION

subject: Commerce
q: Direct sales to consumers by manufacturers is a means of eliminating
explanation: Direct sales to consumers by manufacturers is a means of eliminating middlemen. Middlemen are intermediaries between the manufacturer and the consumer. They can include wholesalers, distributors, brokers, and retailers. By selling directly to consumers, manufacturers can avoid the costs and complications of dealing with these intermediaries.

topics.title: TRADE : HOME TRADE: RETAIL TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: In a hire purchase agreement, the buyer is the
explanation: In a hire purchase agreement, the buyer is referred to as the hirer. The hirer is the person who hires the goods from the owner (lessor) and has an option to buy the goods by paying installments. The hirer has the right to terminate the agreement at any time in the course of the payment period.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: HIRE PURCHASES : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following countries is not a member of the European Union?
explanation: Bolivia is not a member of the European Union. The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states that are located primarily in Europe. Bolivia is a country located in central South America, and it is not part of the EU. Denmark, France, and Greece are all members of the EU.

topics.title: ECONOMIC GROUPING : MEANING AND TYPES SUCH AS ECOWAS, NIGER BASIN COM ETC.

subject: Commerce
q: What is the primary purpose of marine insurance?
options: To protect against financial loss from maritime activities
explanation: The primary purpose of marine insurance is to protect against financial loss that may occur due to maritime activities, including damage to ships, cargo, and liabilities incurred during shipping operations. This coverage is essential for businesses involved in international trade.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is NOT typically covered by a standard marine insurance policy?
options: Loss of profits due to market fluctuations
explanation: A standard marine insurance policy typically covers physical damage to the ship and cargo, as well as liabilities. However, it does not cover loss of profits due to market fluctuations, as this is considered a business risk rather than a maritime risk.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : TERMS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is an advantage of using a cheque?
options: Cheques provide a written record of payment
explanation: Cheques provide a written record of payment, which is an advantage as it helps both the payer and payee keep track of transactions. Unlike cash, which can be lost or forgotten, a cheque leaves a paper trail that can be referenced later.

topics.title: CHEQUE: ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following statements about the disadvantages of cheques is correct?
options: Cheques can be used for online transactions
Cheques can bounce if there are insufficient funds
explanation: Cheques can bounce if there are insufficient funds, which is a significant disadvantage. If the account holder does not have enough money in their account to cover the cheque, it will not be honored by the bank, leading to potential fees and issues for both the payer and payee.

topics.title: CHEQUE: ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: What is the primary goal of customer service in a business?
explanation: The primary goal of customer service is to enhance customer satisfaction by addressing their needs and concerns effectively. Satisfied customers are more likely to return and recommend the business to others.

topics.title: CUSTOMER SERVICES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is NOT a key component of effective customer service?
explanation: Ignoring customer feedback is NOT a key component of effective customer service. Instead, businesses should actively listen to and address customer feedback to improve their services and meet customer needs.

topics.title: CUSTOMER SERVICES

subject: Commerce
q: The following statement about customer service is correct except:
options: Customer service representatives should be knowledgeable about the products.
Customer service is only important for retail businesses.
Good customer service can lead to customer loyalty.
Customer service can impact a company's reputation.
explanation: The statement that customer service is only important for retail businesses is incorrect. Customer service is crucial across all industries, as it affects customer satisfaction and loyalty regardless of the business type.

topics.title: CUSTOMER SERVICES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following actions is considered a best practice in customer service?
options: Following up with customers after a service interaction
explanation: Following up with customers after a service interaction is considered a best practice in customer service as it shows that the business values customer feedback and is committed to improving their experience.

topics.title: CUSTOMER SERVICES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is considered a non-cash means of payment?
explanation: A cheque is a written order directing a bank to pay a specific amount from the writer's account to the person named on the cheque. It is a non-cash means of payment as it does not involve the immediate exchange of cash.

topics.title: MEANS OF PAYMENT

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following statements about electronic funds transfer (EFT) is correct?
options: EFT is only used for international payments.
EFT eliminates the need for physical cheques.
EFT is slower than traditional payment methods.
explanation: Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) allows for the transfer of money from one bank account to another without the need for physical cheques. This makes it a faster and more efficient method of payment compared to traditional methods.

topics.title: MEANS OF PAYMENT

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is NOT a type of negotiable instrument?
explanation: An invoice is a document requesting payment for goods or services and is not a negotiable instrument. Negotiable instruments include promissory notes, bills of exchange, and cheques, which can be transferred from one person to another.

topics.title: NEGOTIABLE INSTRUMENTS

subject: Commerce
q: A cheque is considered a negotiable instrument because it can be transferred by endorsement. Which of the following statements about cheques is correct?
options: A cheque must be signed by the payee.
A cheque can be issued without a date.
A cheque can be crossed to restrict payment.
A cheque does not require a bank to honor it.
explanation: A cheque can be crossed to restrict payment, meaning it can only be deposited into a bank account and cannot be cashed directly. This is a common practice to enhance security. The other statements are incorrect as a cheque must be signed by the drawer, should ideally have a date, and requires the bank to honor it if it is valid.

topics.title: CHEQUE: ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: What is the primary goal of privatization?
options: To enhance efficiency and profitability of businesses
explanation: The primary goal of privatization is to enhance efficiency and profitability of businesses by transferring ownership from the public sector to private entities, which are often more motivated to improve performance and reduce costs.

topics.title: PRIVATIZATION AND NATIONALIZATION

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a potential disadvantage of nationalization?
options: Reduced efficiency due to lack of competition
explanation: A potential disadvantage of nationalization is reduced efficiency due to lack of competition. When industries are nationalized, they may not face the same competitive pressures as private firms, which can lead to complacency and inefficiency.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PUBLIC CORPORATION : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following statements about implied terms in the Sale of Goods Act is correct?
options: Implied terms can only be excluded by express agreement.
Implied terms are not applicable to consumer sales.
Implied terms are automatically included in every sale contract.
Implied terms can only be included if the buyer requests them.
explanation: Implied terms in the Sale of Goods Act can only be excluded by express agreement between the parties involved. This means that if both the buyer and seller agree to exclude certain implied terms, they can do so, but this must be clearly stated in the contract.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: SALES OF GOODS ACT : MEANING AND CONDITIONS

subject: Commerce
q: The Sale of Goods Act implies that goods sold must be of satisfactory quality except when:
options: The goods are sold at a discounted price.
explanation: The Sale of Goods Act implies that goods sold must be of satisfactory quality unless the buyer has inspected the goods and accepted them in their current condition. This means that if the buyer has had the opportunity to examine the goods and chooses to proceed with the purchase, they cannot later claim that the goods are not of satisfactory quality.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: SALES OF GOODS ACT : MEANING AND CONDITIONS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is NOT an implied term under the Sale of Goods Act?
explanation: While the Sale of Goods Act includes several implied terms such as goods being fit for purpose, matching the description, and being of satisfactory quality, there is no specific requirement that goods must be delivered within 30 days. Delivery terms can vary and are typically specified in the contract between the buyer and seller.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: SALES OF GOODS ACT : MEANING AND CONDITIONS

subject: Commerce
q: The outright purchase of one company by another is called
explanation: The outright purchase of one company by another is called an 'Acquisition'. In this process, one company takes over another and establishes itself as the new owner. This is distinct from a merger, where two companies combine to form a new entity.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: MERGER : MEANING AND TYPES OF MERGER

subject: Commerce
q: The marketing concept which begins and ends with satisfying the needs and wants of consumer is called
explanation: The marketing concept which begins and ends with satisfying the needs and wants of consumers is called 'Consumer orientation'. This approach focuses on understanding and meeting the desires of customers, ensuring that products and services are tailored to their preferences.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : THE MARKETING CONCEPT

subject: Commerce
q: A contract made between a ship owner and a merchant for the carriage of cargo is
explanation: A contract made between a ship owner and a merchant for the carriage of cargo is known as a 'charter party'. This is a legal term for a contract between the owner of a vessel and the charterer who rents use of the vessel or a part of its cargo space. The other options listed do not accurately describe this type of contract.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : DOCUMENT USED IN TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: An aspect of marketing that stimulates buying by providing free gifts is
explanation: Sales promotion is an aspect of marketing that stimulates buying by providing free gifts. It includes various short-term incentives to encourage the purchase of a product or service, such as discounts, coupons, and free samples.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

subject: Commerce
q: The two main branches of trade are
explanation: Trade is divided or classified into home and foreign trade.

topics.title: TRADE (1) : HOME TRADE: WHOLESALE TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Retail outlets which sell from specialized vans are called
explanation: Retail outlets that sell from specialized vans are referred to as 'mobile shops'. These shops operate from vehicles and provide convenience by bringing products directly to consumers, often in locations where traditional retail outlets may not be available.

topics.title: TRADE : HOME TRADE: RETAIL TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Entreport trade refers to
explanation: Entreport trade refers to the practice of importing goods with the intention of re-exporting them to another country. This is often done to take advantage of lower tariffs or to serve as a transit point for goods. Therefore, the correct option is 'Importing for re-export'.

topics.title: TRADE : FOREIGN TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Which of these is a compulsory requirement by commercial banks when granting loans to customers ?
explanation: Collateral is a compulsory requirement for commercial banks when granting loans. It serves as security for the loan, ensuring that the bank can recover its funds in case the borrower defaults. This is a common practice in lending to mitigate risk.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES & CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following refers to a price reduction based on the size of order?
explanation: A quantity discount is a price reduction given to customers based on the size of their order. This encourages larger purchases and is a common pricing strategy used by businesses to increase sales volume.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

subject: Commerce
q: The limit of functions, activities and personnel that a supervisor can effectively manage in a business organization is
explanation: The correct answer is 'span of authority'. This term refers to the number of subordinates that a supervisor can effectively manage. A wider span of authority means a supervisor can oversee more employees, while a narrower span allows for more direct supervision.

topics.title: ELEMENTS OF BUSINESS MANAGEMENT : PRINCIPLE

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a creditor legally bound to accept for settlement of a debt ?
explanation: A cheque is a negotiable instrument that a creditor is legally bound to accept for the settlement of a debt. It represents an order to a bank to pay a specific amount from the drawer's account to the payee.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : MEANING AND TYPES OF CONTRACT

subject: Commerce
q:
Given:
#
Opening stock 40,000
Purchases 115,000
Closing stock 60,000
Sales 250,000


What is the ending inventory ?
explanation: The closing stock is also known as stock at close, unused stock or ending inventory. Therefore, the closing stock is #60,000 as stipulated from the question.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : PROFIT

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following have lien on goods in their possession until their commission is paid?
explanation: Factors have a lien on goods in their possession until their commission is paid. A factor is an agent who conducts business under his own name for another party, often taking possession of the goods. In commerce, a lien is a right to keep possession of property belonging to another person until a debt owed by that person is discharged. Therefore, factors have the right to retain possession of the goods until they are paid their commission.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: ADVERTISING : TYPES AND MEDIA

subject: Commerce
q: Product ,pricing, place and promotion are elements of
explanation: Product, pricing, place, and promotion are elements of the marketing mix, which is a foundational concept in marketing that outlines the key components that a company can control to influence consumers' purchasing decisions.

topics.title: ELEMENT OF MARKETING : MARKETING MIX

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is an example of itinerant trading ?
explanation: Itinerant trading refers to businesses that do not have a fixed location and move from place to place. A mobile shop is a clear example of this type of trading, as it operates by traveling to different locations to sell goods.

topics.title: TRADE : HOME TRADE: RETAIL TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following ensures regular transportation of gas and liquid products in all weather conditions?
explanation: The transportation of gas and liquid products in all weather conditions is ensured by pipelines. Pipelines are specially designed for the safe, efficient, and often long-distance transportation of products including natural gas, crude oil, and refined products. They are not affected by weather conditions unlike trains, aeroplanes, and tankers.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : MEANING AND TYPES OF TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: The price charged for leasing a ship is known as
explanation: Freight is the term used to describe the price charged for transporting goods by sea or waterway, which includes leasing a ship. Therefore, the price charged for leasing a ship is known as freight.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : MEANING AND TYPES OF TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following currencies does not belong to any country within ECOWAS?
explanation: The Rand is the currency of South Africa, which is not a member of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS). The other currencies listed (Naira, Cedis and pesewas, Dalasi) belong to countries that are part of ECOWAS. Therefore, the correct option is 'Rand'.

topics.title: ECONOMIC GROUPING : MEANING AND TYPES SUCH AS ECOWAS, NIGER BASIN COM ETC.

subject: Commerce
q: When an insured person admits that he has been indemnified, it means
options: The insurance company has restored him to his former position
The insured has made gains from insurance policy
explanation: When an insured person admits that he has been indemnified, it means 'The insurance company has restored him to his former position'. Indemnification in insurance refers to the compensation provided to the insured for a loss, ensuring that they are returned to the financial state they were in before the loss occurred.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : PRINCIPLES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following has a fixed rate of dividend?
explanation: A preference share has a fixed rate of dividend, which means that the dividend is set at a specific rate and is paid out before any dividends are paid to ordinary shareholders. This is in contrast to ordinary shares, which do not guarantee a fixed dividend.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE : TYPES OF SECURITIES

subject: Commerce
q: The market for short term capital is called
explanation: The market for short-term capital is called the money market. This market deals with financial instruments that have maturities of one year or less, such as Treasury bills and commercial paper, making it distinct from other markets like the stock market, which deals with long-term securities.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : SOURCE OF FINANCE

subject: Commerce
q: In law of contract, what is given in exchange for a promise to perform is
explanation: In contract law, 'consideration' refers to something of value that is exchanged between parties when entering into a contract. It is a fundamental element of a valid contract, as it represents the benefit or value that each party agrees to provide to the other.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: CONTRACT : ELEMENT OF A VALID CONTRACT

subject: Commerce
q: Management can be defined as
options: The number of levels in an organization
explanation: Management can be defined as 'getting things done through people' because it involves coordinating and overseeing the work of others to achieve organizational goals. This definition emphasizes the role of managers in utilizing human resources effectively.

topics.title: ELEMENTS OF BUSINESS MANAGEMENT : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following trends in retailing provides the convenience of shopping without cash
explanation: The trend in retailing that provides the convenience of shopping without cash is the use of credit cards. Credit cards allow consumers to purchase goods or services on credit, meaning they can buy now and pay later. This eliminates the need for carrying cash and provides a convenient and secure method of payment.

topics.title: TRADE (1) : HOME TRADE: RETAIL TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is not a means of controlling credit by the Central Bank?
explanation: The Central Bank controls credit through various means such as fixing the bank rate, open market operations, and special deposits. However, clearing inter-bank indebtedness is not a means of controlling credit by the Central Bank. Clearing inter-bank indebtedness is a process that involves settling debts between banks, which is not a direct method of credit control.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES AND CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following types of cheques is payable on demand?
explanation: A Bearer Cheque is payable on demand to the person who presents it at the bank. It does not require endorsement and can be cashed by anyone holding it. In contrast, a Crossed Cheque is not payable on demand as it must be deposited into a bank account, and an Order Cheque requires endorsement. A Post-dated Cheque is not payable until a specified future date.

topics.title: CHEQUE: TYPES OF CHEQUE

subject: Commerce
q: The following is true about a crossed cheque except:
options: It can only be deposited into a bank account
It is safer than an open cheque
It can be cashed at any bank
It has two parallel lines drawn across it
explanation: A crossed cheque cannot be cashed at any bank; it must be deposited into a bank account. This feature makes it safer than an open cheque, which can be cashed by anyone. The two parallel lines drawn across it indicate that it is a crossed cheque, which restricts its negotiability.

topics.title: CHEQUE: ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a characteristic of the First Tier Security Market?
options: It is a market for trading shares of large, well-established companies.
It is primarily for small and medium enterprises.
It only allows foreign investors to participate.
explanation: The First Tier Security Market is characterized by the trading of shares of large, well-established companies, which are typically listed on the stock exchange. This market provides a platform for these companies to raise capital by issuing shares to the public.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE: FIRST TIER SECURITY MARKET

subject: Commerce
q: The following statements about the First Tier Security Market are correct except:
options: It is regulated by the Securities and Exchange Commission.
It only includes stocks of companies with a market capitalization below a certain threshold.
It allows for the buying and selling of shares.
explanation: The statement that the First Tier Security Market only includes stocks of companies with a market capitalization below a certain threshold is incorrect. In fact, this market includes stocks of large, established companies, which typically have a higher market capitalization.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE: FIRST TIER SECURITY MARKET

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a sole proprietorship?
explanation: A sole proprietorship is characterized by single ownership, meaning that one individual owns and operates the business. This structure does not provide limited liability, as the owner is personally liable for all debts. It also does not have a separate legal entity status, and taxation is generally simpler compared to other business forms.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP : FEATURES AND CHARACTERISTICS

subject: Commerce
q: The following is true about a partnership except
explanation: A partnership is not considered a separate legal entity; instead, it is a collective of individuals who share profits and losses. Partners typically have unlimited liability, meaning they are personally responsible for the debts of the partnership. While a formal agreement is advisable, it is not a strict requirement for the formation of a partnership.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PARTNERSHIP : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following business structures is most suitable for raising large amounts of capital?
explanation: A corporation is most suitable for raising large amounts of capital because it can issue shares to the public, allowing it to attract a large number of investors. This structure also limits the liability of its shareholders, making it an attractive option for investors. Sole proprietorships and partnerships have limitations on capital raising, while LLCs, although flexible, do not have the same capacity as corporations.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVATAGES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following terms refers to the movement of goods from one location to another?
explanation: Logistics is the term that specifically refers to the planning, implementation, and control of the movement of goods and services from the point of origin to the point of consumption. It encompasses transportation, warehousing, and inventory management.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : MEANING AND TYPES OF TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: The following statement about freight transportation is correct except:
options: Freight can be transported by land, sea, or air.
Freight transportation is only applicable to large shipments.
Freight rates can vary based on distance and weight.
Freight includes both raw materials and finished goods.
explanation: The statement that 'Freight transportation is only applicable to large shipments' is incorrect. Freight transportation can apply to both large and small shipments, as it encompasses any goods transported for commercial purposes, regardless of size.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES OF TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a method of transportation that involves the use of pipelines?
explanation: Pipeline transport is a method of transportation that specifically involves the use of pipelines to transport liquids and gases over long distances. It is distinct from rail, air, and maritime transport, which utilize different modes of conveyance.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : MEANING AND TYPES OF TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a primary function of a carrier in transportation?
explanation: The primary function of a carrier in transportation is to transport goods from one location to another. Carriers play a crucial role in the supply chain by ensuring that products reach their destination efficiently and safely.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : MEANING AND TYPES OF TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: The process of containerization primarily benefits which aspect of transportation?
explanation: Containerization benefits transportation by reducing shipping times. It allows for the efficient loading and unloading of goods, minimizes handling, and enables the use of standardized containers that can be easily transferred between different modes of transport.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES OF TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following statements about a charter party is correct?
options: A charter party is a contract for the sale of goods.
A charter party is a contract for the lease of a vessel.
A charter party is a type of insurance policy.
A charter party is a document for customs clearance.
explanation: A charter party is a contract for the lease of a vessel, where the owner of the vessel agrees to rent it to a charterer for a specified period or for a specific voyage. This contract outlines the rights and obligations of both parties involved.

topics.title: TRANSPORTATION: TERMINOLOGIES IN TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: The following is true about the role of carriers in international trade except:
options: They ensure the safety of goods during transit.
explanation: While carriers facilitate global commerce, ensure the safety of goods during transit, and may assist with customs clearance, they do not determine the price of goods. Pricing is typically set by the sellers or manufacturers based on market conditions.

topics.title: TRANSPORTATION: CARRIERS CONTAINERIZATION AND CHARTRR PARTY

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a promissory note?
options: It is a type of insurance policy
It is a written promise to pay a specified amount
It requires collateral to secure the loan
It is a form of equity financing
explanation: A promissory note is a financial instrument that contains a written promise by one party to pay a specified sum of money to another party at a specified future date or on demand. It does not require collateral and is not related to insurance or equity financing.

topics.title: NEGOTIABLE INSTRUMENTS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following tells the customer exactly what has been sold to him, the amount due and any discount granted
explanation: An 'Invoice' is a document that tells the customer exactly what has been sold to them, the amount due, and any discounts granted. It serves as a request for payment and provides a detailed account of the transaction.

topics.title: PURCHASES AND SALES OF GOODS : TERMS OF PAYMENT

subject: Commerce
q: The type of credit offered by commercial banks in Nigeria is
explanation: The type of credit offered by commercial banks in Nigeria is overdraft. An overdraft allows a customer to withdraw more money than they have in their account, up to a certain limit, providing short-term liquidity.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES & CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: Safe guarding the health of the nation is the slogan used by
explanation: The slogan 'Safe guarding the health of the nation' is associated with the National Health Insurance Scheme (NHIS) in Nigeria, which aims to provide accessible and affordable healthcare to citizens, thereby safeguarding public health.

topics.title: LEGAL ASPECT OF BUSINESS: GOVERNMENT REGULATION : CONSUMER PROTECTION

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is not a factor of production?
explanation: Staffing is not traditionally considered a factor of production. The main factors of production are land, labor, capital, and entrepreneurship. Staffing is more related to human resource management rather than a direct factor of production. Therefore, the correct option is 'Staffing'.

topics.title: PRODUCTION : FACTORS OF PRODUCTION

subject: Commerce
q: The Customs and Excise department is responsible for the collection of
explanation: The Customs and Excise department is responsible for the collection of tariffs, which are taxes imposed on imported and exported goods. This is a key function of customs authorities worldwide.

topics.title: TRADE : CUSTOMS, EXCISE, PORTS AUTHORITY ETC.

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following encourages people to save towards owning a house?
explanation: A mortgage bank specializes in providing loans specifically for purchasing real estate. They encourage people to save towards owning a house by offering mortgage products that allow individuals to finance their home purchases. Thus, the correct option is 'Mortgage bank'.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : SOURCE OF FINANCE

subject: Commerce
q: The money market is the
explanation: The money market is primarily concerned with the lending and borrowing of short-term funds, typically with maturities that are less than one year. This includes instruments like Treasury bills, commercial paper, and certificates of deposit. Therefore, the correct option is 'market for short term loans'.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES & CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: When a customer writes a cheque in his own name and withdraws cash with it from his account, he is both the
explanation: When a customer writes a cheque in their own name and withdraws cash, they are acting as the drawer (the person who writes the cheque) and the payee (the person who receives the payment). Therefore, the correct option is 'Drawer and the payee'.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES & CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: When a firm speaks of stock appreciation, it refers to
options: changes in the value of its stock resulting from price movements
increase in the price of its shares quoted on the stock exchange
an unexpected increase in the demand for its stocks
the value of its stocks in the stock exchange
explanation: When a firm speaks of stock appreciation, it refers to changes in the value of its stock resulting from price movements. This appreciation indicates an increase in the market value of the company's shares over time.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE : MEANING, IMPORTANCE AND FUNCTIONS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is an intangible asset of a firm?
explanation: Goodwill is considered an intangible asset of a firm. It represents the value of a company's brand, customer relationships, and other non-physical assets that contribute to its earning potential. Unlike tangible assets, goodwill cannot be physically touched or measured.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : TYPES OF CAPITAL

subject: Commerce
q: The transfer of ownership of a business from government to the public is
explanation: Privatization is the transfer of ownership, property or business from the government to the private sector. The government ceases to be the owner of the entity or business. Communication is the exchange of information or news. Deregulation is the reduction or elimination of government power in a particular industry, usually enacted to create more competition within the industry. Merger is a combination of two things, especially companies, into one.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: PUBLIC CORPORATION : MEANING AND TYPES

subject: Commerce
q: A company has recently advertised an issue of one million ordinary shares of 50 kobo at 65 kobo per share. When the issue is fully paid, the company would collect
explanation: When a company issues one million ordinary shares at 65 kobo per share, the total amount collected when the issue is fully paid is calculated as follows: 1,000,000 shares * 0.65 Naira = #650,000. This represents the total funds raised from the share issue.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : SOURCE OF FINANCE

subject: Commerce
q: The selection and promotion of staff in an organization is a function of the
explanation: The selection and promotion of staff in an organization is primarily the function of the 'Personnel department', also known as Human Resources. This department is responsible for recruiting, training, and managing employees, ensuring that the organization has the right talent to meet its objectives.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: COMMUNICATION : SERVICES

subject: Commerce
q: The fees paid for transporting goods by ship is
explanation: The fees paid for transporting goods by ship are referred to as freight. This term specifically denotes the cost associated with the transportation of goods, while fare typically refers to passenger transport, and demurrage refers to charges for the delay of a ship.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: TRANSPORTATION : DOCUMENT USED IN TRANSPORTATION

subject: Commerce
q: A shop which consists of many stores trading under a single roof and which stocks varieties of goods with each unit having its own head is a
explanation: A departmental store is characterized by having many stores trading under a single roof, each offering a variety of goods and managed independently. This structure allows for a wide range of products and services, making it distinct from other types of retail formats.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: WAREHOUSING: : TYPES & IMPORTANCE

subject: Commerce
q: Most foreign trade transactions are paid for through the use of
options: bank drafts drawn by commercial banks on their foreign branches
letters of credit authenticated by the embassies of the respective countries
explanation: Most foreign trade transactions are typically secured through irrevocable and confirmed letters of credit. This financial instrument provides a guarantee from a bank that a buyer's payment to a seller will be received on time and for the correct amount. The correct option that matches this answer is 'irrevocable and confirmed letters of credit'.

topics.title: TRADE : DOCUMENT USED IN INTERNATIONAL TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: In insurance, risk that have no statistics for their calculations are
explanation: In insurance, risks that have no statistics for their calculations are referred to as un-insurable risks. This is because insurance companies rely on statistical data to calculate the probability of a risk occurring and to determine the premium to be charged. If there are no statistics available, the insurance company cannot accurately assess the risk and therefore, it becomes un-insurable.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: INSURANCE : TERMS

subject: Commerce
q: Net profit is the
options: Total income from the sake of goods
explanation: Net profit is defined as the 'Total income from the sale of goods' minus all expenses incurred in generating that income. It represents the actual profit after all costs have been deducted from revenue, making it a key indicator of a company's profitability.

topics.title: BUSINESS CAPITAL, PROFIT AND TURNOVER : PROFIT

subject: Commerce
q: Agricultural cooperative banks are best classified under
explanation: Agricultural cooperative banks are best classified as specialized banks because they focus on providing financial services specifically to the agricultural sector, unlike commercial banks which serve a broader market.

topics.title: AIDS TO TRADE: BANKING : SERVICES & CHALLENGES

subject: Commerce
q: A bundle of share is called
explanation: A bundle of shares is referred to as 'Stock'. In finance, stock represents ownership in a company and can be traded in the stock market. The term 'stock' encompasses all shares of a company, while 'shares' refer to individual units of ownership.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE : TYPES OF SECURITIES

subject: Commerce
q: A speculator on the stock exchange who sells securities for fear of a fall in their prices is called
explanation: A speculator on the stock exchange who sells securities due to fear of a fall in their prices is referred to as 'A bear'. Bears are investors who believe that the market or a particular stock will decline in value, prompting them to sell their holdings. The correct option is 'A bear', which accurately describes this behavior in the stock market.

topics.title: STOCK EXCHANGE : TYPES OF SECURITIES

subject: Commerce
q: A very effective way to resist a producer's exploitation is to
explanation: Forming a 'trade union' is a very effective way to resist a producer's exploitation. Trade unions are organizations that represent the collective interests of workers, allowing them to negotiate better working conditions, wages, and benefits.

topics.title: TRADE ASSOCIATION : MEANING & FUNCTIONS

subject: Commerce
q: An outright prohibition on the important of goods to a country is referred to as
explanation: An embargo is an official ban on trade or other commercial activity with a particular country. It is an outright prohibition on the import or export of goods to a country. A quota is a limited quantity of a particular product that under official controls can be produced, exported, or imported. A tariff is a tax or duty to be paid on a particular class of imports or exports. Devaluation is the reduction of the value of a currency with respect to those goods, services or other monetary units with which that currency can be exchanged.

topics.title: TRADE ( 2) : FOREIGN TRADE

subject: Commerce
q: The headquarters of the lake Chad Basin Comission is in
explanation: The headquarters of the Lake Chad Basin Commission is located in N'Djamena, which is the capital city of Chad. This organization focuses on the sustainable management of the Lake Chad Basin's resources.

topics.title: ECONOMIC GROUPING : MEANING AND TYPES SUCH AS ECOWAS, NIGER BASIN COM ETC.

subject: Commerce
q: One objective of the European Union is to
options: fight the guilty nation in time of war
eliminate all tariffs and quotas among member countries
fight against child abuse in member countries
explanation: The European Union (EU) is a political and economic union of 27 member states that are located primarily in Europe. One of its main objectives is to promote economic cooperation among member countries. This includes the elimination of tariffs and quotas among member countries to promote free trade. Tariffs and quotas are trade barriers that restrict the free flow of goods and services. Eliminating these barriers allows for a more efficient allocation of resources, leading to increased economic growth and prosperity.

topics.title: ECONOMIC GROUPING : MEANING AND TYPES SUCH AS ECOWAS, NIGER BASIN COM ETC.

subject: Commerce
q: What is the primary purpose of a trade association?
options: To promote the interests of its members
explanation: The primary purpose of a trade association is to promote the interests of its members, which typically include businesses within a specific industry. They provide support, advocacy, and resources to help their members succeed.

topics.title: TRADE ASSOCIATION : MEANING & FUNCTIONS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following best describes a holding company?
options: A company that sells products directly to consumers
A company that owns other companies' outstanding stock
A company that provides financial services to other businesses
A company that operates in multiple industries
explanation: A holding company is defined as a company that owns other companies' outstanding stock. This allows it to control and manage the operations of those companies without necessarily being involved in their day-to-day activities.

topics.title: BUSINESS UNIT: MERGER : MEANING AND TYPES OF MERGER

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following statements about price rings is correct?
options: Price rings are illegal in most countries.
Price rings are used to stabilize prices among competitors.
Price rings are a form of government regulation.
Price rings are only used in the agricultural sector.
explanation: Price rings are agreements among competitors to stabilize prices, often to avoid price wars and ensure a fair market. While they can be controversial, they are not inherently illegal unless they violate antitrust laws.

topics.title: TRADE ASSOCIATION: CARTEL TRUST AND CONSORTIUM

subject: Commerce
q: What is a cartel in the context of trade associations?
options: A group of independent companies that collaborate to control prices
A legal agreement between companies to share resources
A type of cooperative that focuses on community development
explanation: A cartel is formed when independent companies come together to control prices and limit competition in the market. This is often done to maximize profits at the expense of consumers.

topics.title: TRADE ASSOCIATION: CARTEL TRUST AND CONSORTIUM

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following statements about trusts is correct?
options: Trusts are formed to eliminate competition among companies
Trusts are a type of cooperative organization
Trusts are primarily used for charitable purposes
explanation: Trusts are formed when companies come together to eliminate competition and control market prices. This practice can lead to monopolistic behavior, which is often regulated by law.

topics.title: TRADE ASSOCIATION: CARTEL TRUST AND CONSORTIUM

subject: Commerce
q: The primary purpose of a consortium is to
options: Share resources and expertise for a common goal
explanation: A consortium is formed when multiple organizations collaborate to share resources and expertise to achieve a common goal, such as a large project or research initiative. Unlike cartels or trusts, the focus is on cooperation rather than competition.

topics.title: TRADE ASSOCIATION : MEANING & FUNCTIONS

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following best describes the concept of authority in a business structure?
options: The ability to influence others without any formal power
The formal right to make decisions and enforce obedience
The skill of managing conflicts within a team
explanation: Authority in a business context refers to the formal right to make decisions and enforce obedience within an organization. It is typically granted through a hierarchy and is essential for effective management and leadership.

topics.title: STRUCTURE OF BUSINESS: POWER AND AUTHORITY

subject: Commerce
q: The following statement about power in a business organization is correct except:
options: Power can be derived from position within the organizational hierarchy.
Power can be exercised through personal influence.
explanation: While power can be derived from position and can be exercised through personal influence, it is not always associated with authority. Individuals can have power through expertise or relationships even without formal authority.

topics.title: STRUCTURE OF BUSINESS: POWER AND AUTHORITY

subject: Commerce
q: In a flat organizational structure, the level of authority is typically
explanation: In a flat organizational structure, authority is distributed among employees, which encourages collaboration and faster decision-making. This contrasts with hierarchical structures where authority is concentrated at the top.

topics.title: STRUCTURE OF BUSINESS: POWER AND AUTHORITY

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a promissory note?
options: It is an unconditional promise to pay a specified amount.
It requires a third party to guarantee the payment.
It can only be issued by banks.
explanation: A promissory note is a written, unconditional promise made by one party to pay a specified sum of money to another party at a specified future date or on demand. This characteristic distinguishes it from other financial instruments.

topics.title: NEGOTIABLE INSTRUMENTS

subject: Commerce
q: The following is true about a cheque except
options: It is a written order to a bank to pay a specified amount.
It requires a signature from the payee.
It can be transferred to another party.
explanation: A cheque requires a signature from the drawer (the person writing the cheque), not the payee (the person receiving the cheque). Therefore, option 3 is incorrect.

topics.title: CHEQUE: ADVANTAGES AND DISADVANTAGES

subject: Commerce
q: Which of the following terms refers to the person who receives payment in a negotiable instrument?
